programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee tries to help his sister, who believes she may have murdered someone - but is too traumatised and disorientated to remember exactly what happened. As the team investigates the connected case of a sailor's death, Tony and Abby find themselves distracted by relationship problems. Drama, starring Sean Murray, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Two FBI agents try to seize control of the team's investigation into the discovery of a perfectly preserved body hidden in a chimney at a marine base. Reluctant to be ousted from the case, Gibbs agrees to work with them - and learns the victim was a serial killer responsible for the deaths of 14 people. Meanwhile, McGee's literary efforts lead to discord with the others

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to find out who was responsible for sabotaging a robotic vehicle that caused the death of a Navy lieutenant and almost killed Abby. Meanwhile, Ziva's suspicions are aroused by the discovery that Tony is repeatedly visiting the hospital. Drama, starring Pauley Perrette, Michael Weatherly, Cote de Pablo and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The discovery of a dead marine intelligence officer in a motel room sets the team off on a hunt for her killer. Although the town's authorities have allegedly already investigated the crime, Gibbs is determined to conduct his own thorough inquiries - which may lead him to a terrorist cell. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team tries to find the culprit behind an ambush on a vehicle transporting marines in the desert, which resulted in the death of one man and abduction of two others. Kensi and Deeks bond as they track the SUV that took the missing soldiers, and Sam risks using a compromised cover identity to meet a former contact in a bid to discover if drug cartels were involved. Meanwhile, Nate returns from his mystery assignment

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval commander's 14-year-old daughter is kidnapped and buried alive in side a wooden box, prompting Callen and the team to race to her rescue to unravel clues that lead to a man who has been imprisoned for carrying out three similar crimes. Chris O'Donnell stars

Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine major is found dead in an underground maintenance room, having scratched out one of his own eyes - and the presence of a webcam suggests someone watched him die. The team investigates with the help of Lt Col Hollis Mann, and links the case to wanted terrorist Mamoun Sharif, an old adversary who is threatening to unleash a chemical attack

programme.desc: A gambler is killed in a hit-and-run accident, and the investigation uncovers the fact that the man's teacher wife was having an affair with one of her students. The case takes a further complex turn when Cerreta and Logan suspect the widow may have manipulated her young lover into committing the crime

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A covert identity created for one of Gibbs' undercover cases 20 years previously resurfaces after a murder victim is found to be using the alias. Meanwhile, Bishop becomes anxious at the thought of having to work incognito in the future. Starring Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: CD goes undercover in a convalescence home to investigate a mysterious death. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine lieutenant's body falls from the sky and the team links the man's death to the disappearance of a Navy chaplain thought to have been kidnapped by rebels working with Colombian crime cartels. Ziva and Tony are dispatched to conduct a rescue operation and enlist the help of former Interpol agent Monique Lisson, while Gibbs discovers there was more to the chaplain's outreach mission than meets the eye. Crime drama, guest starring Karina Lombard (The L Word) and Jamie Lee Curtis (A Fish Called Wanda)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Hanna and the team try to track down a group of terrorists responsible for a double murder - a search complicated by the discovery that the killers have undergone facial reconstructive surgery in a bid to evade justice

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry and Mickey investigate an armed robbery on a shop, and Matt Hinckley's arrival as CPS lawyer at Sun Hill prompts further heart-searching for Emma and Will. John Heaton zealously pursues his zero tolerance policy, while a hung-over Dale discovers Kezia doesn't remember their kiss from the night before. Angela Lonsdale guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Heaton's zero tolerance policy on drugs causes tensions at all levels within his staff, but produces an unexpected lead when a suspected baron's house is searched. Dan and Will interview a boxer they know from the club about a case of vandalism and hatch a plot to keep him out of trouble. Terry has his hands full minding a potentially dangerous prisoner who is about to be released and getting involved with his estranged family once more

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Roger Valentine is paired with Lewis on his return to work and proves to be a bit heavy-handed dealing with a stalker who terrorised a female transsexual, so he's delighted to learn his new partner is prepared to cover for him. Zain gets a lucky break when Dale and Reg bring in a drunk driver who turns out to have connections with shady club owner Kristen Shaw. Terry's bridge-building with Helen suffers a setback

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When Terry's daughter is found to be involved in the local drugs scene, the detective willingly offers to go undercover to find the dealer, but Zain's off-the-record activities with Kristen Shaw lead the operation into an embarrassing fiasco. Dan damages a shoulder while arresting a pair of motorbike thieves, and the boxing gym's injury specialist offers him an illegal injection. Gary Webster guest stars

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Robert serves on the Re-Vaccination Committee. When Emmaline comes to the Bramwells' house for help, they suspect she may suffer a reaction to Finn's vaccination experiments

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Charity Wakefield and Hattie Morahan lead the cast in Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel about a voyage of sexual and romantic discovery. Following the death of her husband, Mrs Dashwood and her daughters are forced to give up their home and move to a small cottage in Devonshire. Elinor is disappointed to leave her old home after falling in love with Edward Ferrars, her sister-in-law's brother, but looks forward to a promised visit. Marianne quickly finds she has an admirer in Colonel Brandon but is swept of her feet by neighbour Willoughby

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Colonel Brandon is called away on urgent business, leaving Marianne free to fall blissfully in love with Willoughby - until he too suddenly leaves for London. Meanwhile, Elinor has a disappointing meeting with Edward, but the reasons for his distant behaviour become clear when Sir John's niece Lucy Steele entrusts her with a shocking secret

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Marianne's health deteriorates as Colonel Brandon reveals the full extent of Willoughby's betrayal. Meanwhile, Edward states his intentions to honour his engagement to Lucy Steele, despite being disinherited by his mother as a result. Charity Wakefield and Dominic Cooper star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor's continuing campaign against bureaucrats finds another victim - a health and safety inspector who comes to check on the compost heap at the bottom of the Meldrews' allotment. Christmas special from 1991, with guest star Eric Idle

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: It is work experience day, but Bren cannot show the new girl a typical routine in the canteen because the new boy has blocked the front of the cooker with his ladder - which the bread delivery woman refuses to go under. Canteen comedy, with Victoria Wood, Thelma Barlow, Sue Cleaver and Shobna Gulati

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Jean vents her anger on the customers, while Tony considers asking Bren to accompany him on a trip to Marbella. Petula returns with interesting news, and Anita celebrates the second week of her relationship with Pedro. Victoria Wood stars

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Bren is so excited about her forthcoming holiday with Tony she even considers buying some new clothes, and while a forlorn Stan tells everyone his sad news, Petula gets a little hot under the collar in her caravan. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Julie Walters

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The local Blainthorp Music Festival celebrates its 50th birthday, bringing with it fierce competition. The sleuth is called in to investigate why competitors have mysteriously started to lose their voices. Light-hearted drama, with Patricia Routledge, Dominic Monaghan and Derek Benfield

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Nurse Crane and Trixie investigate when a mother abandons her children at the clinic, fearing they were being abused or neglected. Her husband admits that she has been behaving strangely lately, and when she is referred to a neurologist by Dr Turner, the truth proves devastating for the whole family. Christopher admits that Alexandra has been unhappy since his divorce, and Violet and Fred organise a beauty contest to raise money for charity. Valerie and Magda both sign up and quickly become close friends - leading to the sharing of a terrible secret

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: As Alexa's birthday approaches, Kieran gives her an early present - the case of a plastic surgeon obsessed with getting her own cosmetic procedures who died after self-administering an anti-wrinkle drug she had developed. Detective drama, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The detective angers his superiors when he tries to connect a member of Edinburgh's criminal fraternity to the shooting of two boys and a teacher at a local college. One of the victims turns out to be the son of Rebus's cousin, giving Templar the perfect excuse to take him off the case. However, the copper is determined to trace a schoolgirl he is certain holds the key to the mystery. Ken Stott stars

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: The maverick High Court Judge's daughter is arrested during a demonstration, presenting him with the challenge of getting her off the hook without falling foul of powerful enemies he has made in his single-minded push to the top of the legal profession. Martin Shaw, Donald Sinden and Caroline Langrishe star

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The trial of a child murderer Pascoe investigated collapses, and the development has a significant impact on his state of mind. As he struggles to deal with the news, the detective becomes increasingly embroiled in the lives of one of the victim's families, against his partner's advice. Drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

With guest star Peter Polycarpou

programme.desc: It's the staff Christmas party and everyone is in high spirits, except Josh, who has been delayed saving a child's life. As a result, his wife Colette ends up dancing with Simon all night, and before long their flirtation turns into something a little more serious. Unfortunately, Josh has already seen the pair together and realised his wife looks happier than she has done in ages

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As Phil visits his poorly son in Wales, Sam agrees to let Stuart move in with her. Kezia is furious when the case against rapist Keith Durante is withdrawn on a technicality and resolves to put pressure on his wife to admit he was lying

David Rintoul stars, with Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: David gives an old friend a shattering piece of advice, but struggles to practise what he preaches. Drama, starring Haydn Gwynne, Gary Mavers and Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A missing teenager's parents make a televised appeal for information on her whereabouts, but Paige rules the only witness who comes forward is unfit to give evidence. Drama, starring Nigel Havers and Jane Gurnett.

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: William makes plans to relax on a day's fishing. However, Hester and her mother want to join him, and he fears their presence will jeopardise his enjoyment of the trip. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.desc: A councillor trying to win the election by attracting the OAP vote incurs Diana's wrath. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.desc: Yvette and Edith both question René about the whereabouts of the gold, while the Colonel and Gruber search the café for the missing Enigma machine

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly buys an old lawnmower from Auntie, which Compo and Clegg promptly help him to sell. Meanwhile, Marina generates gossip with her pastry purchase. Comedy, starring Frank Thornton, Bill Owen and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are asked to restore an ornate water garden cascade in the grounds of a health spa, where they are invited to join owner Frances Caldecott and her children for dinner. That evening, the duo discover Frances has been strangled and her jewellery stolen, and set out to catch the culprit. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Detective Inspector Jack Mooney and his team are tested when a billionaire's fiancée falls from a balcony the day before her lavish wedding. Everything points to suicide - except for the fact that the victim only painted one fingernail. With guest appearances by Denis Lawson, Elizabeth Berrington and Jemima Rooper

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The veteran detectives investigate the murder of a palaeontologist at the Natural History Museum, discovering the victim was a respected but outspoken scientist with a knack for rubbing people up the wrong way. When it comes out he was strongly opposed to the museum's sponsorship deal with a large fuel company, the cops soon have plenty of suspects. Amanda Redman stars, with guest appearances by Trevor Bannister, Lucy Brown and Natasha Little

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Irish paramilitary leader Patrick McCann promises to give Tom information on a planned attack by Middle Eastern terrorists on a British nuclear power station - but not without a catch. In return for the tip-off, MI5 must allow McCann's group to go unwatched for 30 hours. Lorcan Cranitch guest stars, with Matthew Macfadyen, Peter Firth and Esther Hall

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Harry and Nikki are close by when an RAF helicopter crashes into a detention centre for failed asylum seekers. When it seems the military are trying to hush up the incident they each open their own investigations - to prove the pilot was not at fault for the crash and identify a mysterious girl who fled the site amid the carnage. Tom Ward and Emilia Fox star

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A man arrives in an East Anglian village with his sister Joanna to convalesce following a flying accident - but the residents' warm welcome isn't all it seems. Joan Hickson stars as the elderly sleuth, with Andrew Bicknell and Sabina Franklyn

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: DNA tests on a suspected robber prove his innocence but also reveal his father was an unnamed vagrant strangled on a tube train 15 years earlier. The team reopens the case, going in pursuit of the victim's other family members - including the man they believe to be his brother - while Brian questions the homeless community, only to fall foul of a gang of robbers. Peter Davison and Keith Allen guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The Secretary of State for Northern Ireland and his two daughters are killed by a car bomb and a British Army base is attacked. Amid a national outcry, an Irish terrorist group deny responsibility for the second bombing and a Bosnian war criminal is spotted entering the country, prompting the team to suspect a revenge campaign is under way. Matthew Macfadyen, Natasha Little and Keeley Hawes star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The discovery of a child's mutilated body in a river forces Leo to face a ghost from his past. Questions are raised as to whether the victim was murdered in a ritual killing, and evidence points the team in the direction of a nearby church. Meanwhile, Nikki investigates the apparently suspicious death of a priest from a local Catholic boys' school

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Kezia and Smithy work together on a courier van hijack and soon get a result, but later find themselves in trouble for scaring one of Keith Durante's rape victims. James Tennant's fiancee turns up at the station with news that he has evidence of the whereabouts of his missing daughter Amy, but it's clear that seven months of frantic searching have left him a broken man

programme.title: Pobol y Rhondda
programme.desc: Siôn Tomos Owen goes back to his old school to celebrate Ysgol Gymraeg Ynyswen's 65th birthday. He meets one of the school's first pupils before enjoying a cup of tea in the famous Carpaninis cafe with Welsh-language teacher Julie MacMillan and has a picnic up on the Bwlch with Ffion Evans

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the opening fixtures of the Premier League season, including Liverpool v Leeds United at Anfield and Fulham v Arsenal at Craven Cottage. Leeds were playing their first top-flight match since 2004, but could not have faced a sterner test against the champions, who last lost in the league at Anfield in April 2017. Meanwhile, Fulham also faced a tough challenge on their return to the league as they took on their London rivals. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by Robert Buckland MP, justice secretary, Rachel Reeves MP, shadow chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, Simon Coveney TD, Irish foreign minister and Dr Maria Van Kerkhove, WHO Technical Lead on Covid-19. The News review is with Alison Philips, editor of the Daily Mirror and Adam Fleming, BBC Newscast presenter

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: A Great North Run Celebration
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents a look back at highlights of through the years on the day the 40th staging of the iconic half-marathon was scheduled to take place. Jeanette Kwakye meets some of those who would have been running this year, including NHS staff, and the achievements of those who have raised money for charity are celebrated

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters explore a bungalow in the village of Chelsfield and a semi-detached house in Erith, both in the south east of London. Plus, a shop with plenty of flats above

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Mark Stacey and Gary Pe help two teams look for items at Stamford Meadows in Lincolnshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Grimsthorpe castle to find out about its royal collection

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is in Plymouth, where 400 years ago a group of Christians boarded the Mayflower for a journey across the Atlantic that changed American history. With music by Seth Lakeman and Russell Watson

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore finds a pair of seats, an Edwardian armchair and an old typewriter at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Castleford Tigers v Hull FC (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens. The match was originally scheduled for August, but had to be postponed when a number of Hull players and staff tested positive for Covid-19, but now they have recovered the team will have their eyes on the trophy they won back-to-back in 2016 and 2017. The Tigers are looking to win this trophy for the first time since 1986 and avenge a 28-12 defeat at the hands of the Airlie Birds at this stage of last season's competition

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson assists a couple who have a £500,000 budget and a dream of leaving their city life in Bristol behind them and finding their perfect country home in Monmouthshire. While in the county, keen archaeologist Jules visits the village of Trellech, where local excavation efforts are unearthing what could be the largest medieval settlement in Wales.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are in Stoke-on-Trent to learn about an ambitious scheme to bring the countryside into the heart of the city. Margherita finds out what it takes to move a stretch of the River Trent, while Matt meets the next generation of nature lovers as he glories in mud with some of the city's youngest residents and their parents. In the light of the coronavirus pandemic, Tom Heap investigates whether the UK is sufficiently armed against further threats of diseases that spread from animals to humans, and Adam Henson meets another set of regional competitors vying to be crowned national One Man and His Dog champion

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition commemorating the 80th anniversary of the Battle of Britain and the Blitz. Combining stories from all over the country with a special event filmed at Biggin Hill Airfield, this episode tells the story of a defining moment in Britain's war, through the people and objects who were there. Fiona begins on board the iconic Supermarine Spitfire, flying over the Weald of Kent. Then, she and the team uncover the stories from family mementos, including a scramble bell that got the pilots into the air, ops room models that tracked their every move and a steering column from a British fighter shot down in combat

programme.title: Extinction: The Facts
programme.desc: With a million species at threat, David Attenborough explores the science of extinction and how this has consequences for everyone. The programme examines why biodiversity loss is happening and what it means - not just for the natural world, but for all mankind. The loss of biodiversity threatens food and water security, undermines humans' ability to control the climate, and places humans at greater risk of pandemics. Leading experts including Sir Robert Watson and plant scientist Prof Kathy Willis explain how all groups in the natural world are in decline, and that extinction is now happening up to 100 times faster than the natural evolutionary rate

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Robin meets Raphael for a drink, and he explains more about Freddie's bullying of Rhiannon, which gives Geraint a strong motive for Chiswell's murder. They are interrupted by a call from Matthew, which Robin blocks before revealing that they have separated. The police show Robin and Strike CCTV footage of Kinvara at Paddington Station the night before Chiswell died, while Tegan reveals that she previously witnessed Kinvara hit Chiswell over the head with a hammer. Crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling that chronicle cases investigated by private detective Cormoran Strike. Starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Micah Richards to review the latest Premier League fixtures on the opening weekend of the season, featuring West Bromwich Albion v Leicester City at the Hawthorns and Tottenham Hotspur v Everton at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: After losing their opening two games of the season, West Ham United's women's team are preparing to take on their bogey team Manchester City, who are packed full of England superstars. Within their own team, club captain Gilly Flaherty starts thinking about what life might be like when her football career comes to an end, French superstar Kenza Dali opens up about her mother's illness, and there is a rare appearance on the bench for academy player Victoria - who is still working hard on her GCSE exams. Narrated by Jaime Winstone

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters all explore mid-terraces as Martin Roberts visits Burton upon Trent, Dion Dublin takes a trip to Merseyside and Martel Maxwell is in Bromley, Kent

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Members of the England Illegal Money Lending team launch an early-morning operation against suspected loan sharks, and share one of the strangest places they have found hidden cash

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets a Merseyside tenant in poor health who is trapped in a damp rented property, while a Birmingham resident has vanished into thin air - leaving their home empty

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special show to commemorate the 80th anniversary of the Battle of Britain. Charlie Ross visits the RAF Museum and pays his respects to pilots at the Battle of Britain Memorial

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama set during the Second World War, following the lives and loves of women who decide to serve their country by joining the Women's Land Army. New girl Connie Carter causes mayhem from the moment she arrives, Finch desperately tries to save his beloved pet pig, and a visit from a wealthy American industrialist spells intrigue at the Manor House. Starring Becci Gemmell, Jo Woodcock and Selene Hizli

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore what Britons ate during wartime and discover how some of the UK's favourite dishes have evolved from rationing. The duo also meet army cooks to find out what troops on the front line eat today, and prepare rabbit stew and carrot cake

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a sociable couple find a country home in Bedfordshire with enough room for them, their dogs and their very own bespoke garden pub, on a £500,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson meet Esme and Richard, who live in Rugby with their seven pets and want an English country garden with areas for the animals and an outdoor cinema

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, tackling a wooden trunk, a memorial trophy and a gyroscopic toy cyclist

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Investigating one of the world's most brutal trades - the buying and selling of human organs. The programme meets the African migrants who have been exploited for their body parts by criminal gangs. Some have agreed to sell a kidney to finance their journey to Europe, only to be ripped off by the traffickers after the operation. Other victims have their organs taken without consent. Panorama also tracks down the criminals running the trade to find out how they arrange illegal operations in hospitals and clinics

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle packs as much as she can, having told Gray she is going on a last-minute holiday with her family - but he remains suspicious. Tina and Frankie grow closer when they go for a drink at the Vic after work. But why is Frankie so interested in the Carters? Max is happy his divorce from Rainie has finally come through and thanks Ian for helping him out, telling him to keep £2,000 of the money he owes him. The rest, however, he wants back now. Ben takes Callum's advice and asks Phil what to do about the situation with Danny

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by actress and presenter Mel Giedroyc, heavyweight boxer David Haye, actor-musician Martin Kemp and comedian Romesh Ranganathan. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory and the dubious honour of being dubbed host Rob Brydon's liar of the week

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: One-off edition of the observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. The programmes examines how long-serving and newly-qualified Northumbria Police officers respond to the changing world of modern crime, including managing online sex offenders and the threat of firearms. In one of Northumbria's newest departments - the Paedophile Online Investigation Team - Detective Constable Dave Kernaghan is managing a huge rise in new cases, but as he continues to investigate the escalating number of jobs, the work itself takes a toll as he assesses the impact this is having on his own life

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Idris Elba proves he is a dab hand at everything, Scribbler P returns for a lesson on blackface, and Mozart invents a new hit

programme.title: Yung Filly's Celeb Lock-In
programme.desc: Celebrity game show presented by the viral comedian. Featuring prank calls, exclusive house tours and lots of chaos. In this first episode, Filly is joined by EastEnders star Maisie Smith and her mum Julia. Contains some strong language

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with broadcaster and journalist Emma Barnett and stand-up Tom Allen joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team assess properties about to be auctioned in Caerphilly in Wales, Bootle on Merseyside, and Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in Belfast show off a collection of designer goods they seized from a suspected paramilitary drug dealer, and a man who stole from his girlfriend has his assets sold off

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright reports on a Liverpool street with an infestation of vermin and a rented property above a row of shops that housing officers consider unsafe for habitation

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams competing in Builth Wells, Powys. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors proceedings and also learns about Wales' washing machine industry

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Bea falls for an Italian PoW, and Connie flirts with a bashful piano player while entertaining the troops. Meanwhile, Martin discovers he has detached retinas following his accident, and Lady Hoxley's sister Diana snags herself a wealthy American admirer while helping Jack search for his grandson. Second World War drama, starring Jo Woodcock

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore Britain's fondness for picnics, preparing an alfresco lunch at Beaufort Polo Club in the Cotswolds. They also discover how the habit of outdoor dining in summer evolved into its current form, celebrate snacks such as Cornish pasties and Scotch eggs, and bake a Dundee cake

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple living in London's Docklands to find a home with some green space in Buckinghamshire's Chiltern Hills, and pays a visit to the Henley Regatta

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team tries to transform a Reading garden to make it safe for children - despite the fact that no landscaper has been willing to take on the broken slate and rotten decking

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Experts restore a tarnished rugby trophy, a hand-operated Victorian sewing machine, and a 1930s accordion, which has 64 individual components in need of repair

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: At the seaside, Mitch realises something is wrong with Chantelle, but rather than open up to her dad, she simply tells him she is missing the kids. Determined to help, he heads back to Walford to fetch them - but his plan soon backfires. Meanwhile, Ian makes a bid to become chairman of the council planning committee, and Ben fills Jay in on his plan to set up Danny

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Manchester single mothers Hayley and Megan join forces to help improve each other's diet, and turn to Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin for help in finding healthy meals all of their children will enjoy. Chris also visits a butcher to report on the importance of buying locally produced meat and asks members of the public what they think of some of the lesser known, cheaper cuts

programme.title: The Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Actor Ewan McGregor and his brother Colin, an RAF pilot, retell the story of the 1940 air battle that proved to be a turning point in the Second World War. The pair get hands-on experience of flying period fighter aircraft across the skies of England, giving Colin an opportunity to contrast his training in modern combat techniques with the skills required to master the Spitfire's less sophisticated technology. The duo also meet some of those who participated in the conflict itself

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Three more patients undergo treatment for skin conditions with the help of leading medical experts. Max was left with a scab on top of his head caused by sunburn six years ago, Eva suffers from a condition that causes excessive sweating, and Alix cannot bear to look at herself in the mirror because of her acne

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: The final batch of patients undergoing life-changing treatment for skin conditions share their stories. Hannah has had a severe case of eczema since she was two years old, Esiri suffers with hyperpigmentation that over-the-counter products have done nothing for, and Scot has become increasingly sensitive about his psoriasis over the course of his life

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a mortgage advisor, a mother and daughter, and a print manager

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter G. Lorraine Pascale cooks pan-fried mascarpone gnocchi with dreamy basil pesto, and Rachel Khoo serves grapefruit meringue tartlets

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani goes back to her Yorkshire roots exploring the lesser-known River Burn in the glorious Swinton estate. She discovers why people seem to have fallen in love with fishing since lockdown and how the humble willow is the perfect weapon against riverbank erosion. Meanwhile, Adam Henson meets this year's One Man and His Dog competitors from Northern Ireland and Wales, and John Craven joins Cerys Matthews and Simon King for the 2020 Photographic Competition

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reaps harvest from his vegetable garden, plants up a seasonal pot and gives a masterclass on how to make the perfect compost. Adam Frost travels to York to meet a couple who took on 20 acres of land and filled it with flowers, and in Walsall cameras visit a garden planted with exotic palms and foliage that has made a positive contribution to the owner's mental health. Plus, another chance to see a collection of late summer flowering bergamot plants in Wales and the zoologist in Lancashire who has spent his life exploring the microscopic insects that live in the soil

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: The presenters look to the future. George Anderson plants up a large container with what he calls a 'bulb lasagne', using different kinds of bulbs to produce layers of colour in the spring. Carole Baxter shows different methods of propagating new plants for next year, Chris Beardshaw plants a new border for year-round colour and Kirsty Wilson reveals why green roofs are the next big thing

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of simple comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the opening episode, Mary heads back to the city where she learnt to cook, Paris, where she visits one of the city's creperies to finesse her technique. Later, inspired by pastries in Parisian patisserie windows, she makes her own brioche and apple frangipane tart

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King travel around Asia exploring the roots of some of their favourite dishes from the continent. They begin in Hong Kong, where they learn about the versatility of the wok in Cantonese cuisine at a street-food stall, while breakfast reveals a surprising legacy of British rule, before the duo are sent on their way with a traditional Chinese good luck ceremony

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: As a child, Alistair McGowan loved school lunches, especially the puddings. His mother's cooking was equally memorable and innovative, providing plenty of inspiration for the dishes that Brian Turner creates for Alistair. Together they revisit evocative places from Alistair's childhood, from Worcestershire's plum orchards to his old school, where sights and smells take him straight back to his youth. In his childhood home, we discover how Alistair realised his gift for impersonation as he switches effortlessly into some of the voices that helped him make his name

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: Ashley John-Baptiste meets a group of people working to clean up their neighbourhood, and Rev Kate Bottley goes in search of inner peace on a wire high above the cliffs of Anglesey

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Bill Bailey narrates this natural history documentary telling the story of some of nature's most extraordinary partnerships, revealing what sometimes happens when animals need an itch scratching, help getting into food or home security assistance. The answer is, to find a partner - and not necessarily one of the same species. Highlights include hippos joining forces with fish for a full-body exfoliation and ravens inviting wolverines over for some bone-crunching assistance with their dinner

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by Swedish Academy Award-winning actress Ingrid Bergman, exploring the milestones of her life and career, including her appearances in Casablanca and Spellbound. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Casablanca (1942)
programme.desc: The American expat owner of a Moroccan nightclub is reunited with his old flame during the Second World War. Now married to a Czech freedom fighter, she needs his help to get them safely out of the country before they are captured by the Nazis - but the former lovers also have to wrestle the temptation to rekindle their simmering romance. Drama, with Humphrey Bogart, Ingrid Bergman, Claude Rains and Paul Henreid

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Highlights of the past two series of the motoring show, including the time the team took a Yorkshire road trip in the latest sports cars from Ferrari, Aston Martin and Porsche. Plus, Paddy McGuinness building his own luxurious off-roader from scratch and Chris Harris putting the Rolls-Royce Cullinan through its paces

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones cast their eyes over the final batch of business pitches of the current series. Stepping into the arena are an inventor who has come up with an unusual way of controlling household draughts, a businessman who claims there is money to be made in encouraging dogs to eat bugs, a fitness teacher who launches into a shimmying session, and the creator of a high-end garment bag. Evan Davis oversees proceedings as the Dragons decide if any of these pitches are worthy of investment

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for brown trout on the river Test in Hampshire, using a Victorian fly-casting technique pioneered in the area as well as an authentic 19th-century rod. The Mayflies are hatching, and the famously short-lived insects inspire some thoughts on the ephemeral nature of life, and what they would do if they only had 24 hours left

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: Harry Hill casts a comical eye over the history of police dramas, from the early days of Dixon of Dock Green to acclaimed modern shows like Line of Duty. He examines the familiar tropes that audiences know and love, as well as drawing attention to a few that have escaped audience's attention

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker revisits documentaries exploring the commodification of forbidden desires, taking in subjects ranging from the porn industry to the US opioid crisis. He is reunited with porn star JJ, who has turned his back on his old career to begin a new life in eastern Europe, and drug addict Katilia, who is still struggling to turn her life around

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam takes two of her friends to meet with an attorney specialising in divorces, calls Jeff over to help assemble a chinchilla cage, and meets one of Frankie's new friends in surprising circumstances

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam has to persuade a reluctant Phil to give a sample of her DNA, receives some bad news from an orthopaedic specialist, and has a spectacular blow-up with Max

programme.title: Radio 2 Live at Home
programme.desc: Specially recorded performances from and interviews performers

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from the second match of the three-game series, which came from Emirates Old Trafford

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Claude begins to teach Peter how to use his powers, and Hiro is pursued by a mysterious figure as he continues his quest to find the sword. Meanwhile, Claire has some success in the hunt for her birth mother and Niki is offered help from a psychiatrist. Guest starring Christopher Eccleston (Doctor Who)

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Hiro attempts to make his father and sister understand his newly discovered powers, and Claire's search for her birth mother leads her to a Texas trailer park. Meanwhile, Peter's faith in those closest to him is tested when Claude shows him how to harness his abilities. Christopher Eccleston, George Takei and Jessalyn Gilsig star

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Most people who go missing are reported to police within a few hours, but for those who live on the streets it isn't always so straightforward. Aaron hasn't been seen for two months when the homeless healthcare centre that usually treats him notifies Gloucestershire Constabulary that he is a missing person. Officers scour the streets and homeless shelters but can find no trace of him. The town's rough sleepers know him well, but no one has any idea what may have happened to him. It's not long before another drug user is added to the missing priority list - James, who is of no fixed address, has discharged himself from hospital against medical advice. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Joanna, Robert and their son Morgan have just moved into their brand-new house in Wrexham. On finding the prospect of sorting out the bare garden quite daunting they call upon Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich to help clear the builders' rubble and provide some ideas. The family has a £6,500 budget to create a garden from scratch that delivers a real 'wow' factor, incorporating dramatic planting, big architectural features and a swanky hot tub area

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sisters Linda Atkinson and Pauline Murphy from Merseyside arrive with their mother's pride and joy - a chair created for the investiture ceremony of the Prince of Wales in 1969. One of 4,000 made and later auctioned off to the general public, it is now in need of urgent attention, spurring restorer Will Kirk into action. Also being given a much-needed makeover are an air-powered toy car and a RNLI donation box. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham counts butterflies to better understand how to attract them to his garden. George and Gill Anderson are in the allotment making the most of August's produce

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer return to Scotland for their latest fishing trip. Having failed to catch a salmon in the River Tay, they hope to have more luck on the Tweed. They begin their journey at the isolated Bemersyde Estate before moving on to the Bridge Pool at Kelso, one of the most famous salmon fishing spots in Scotland. They also try their hand at boat fishing and receive some mystical predictions from a familiar face

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: The antiques-buying challenge reaches Derby, where Paul Martin teaches Kate Dumelow and Lucy Foster-Brown the tricks of the trade to see who can make the most profit

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Anna hides her pregnancy from Henry, while Olivia decides to tell Matt the truth about Georgie's parentage. Return of the romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A portrait of the wildlife living on the Dragon Mountains in South Africa, charting the fortunes of baboons, vultures and jackals as they survive on the slopes. Facing constant challenges from birth, the eland antelope climb high to find food, but must avoid wildfires, freezing rain and starvation along the way

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: A team of marine experts reveal the hidden stories of our planet's last true wilderness - its oceans. Here, they investigate how the predatory Humboldt squid is invading the sea

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the RAF Museum at Hendon in London, where items up for auction include a nautilus shell and some Superman film memorabilia. With antiques experts Michael Baggott and Mark Stacey

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Shaun Keaveny, Rachel Parris and Jan Ravens test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheepdog trainers and farmers James Read from Lincolnshire and Cheshire based James Gilman compete in Sheep Dog Trials at The Hope Agricultural Show in the Peak District

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Journalists and broadcasters Grace Dent and Amol Rajan join experts Catherine Southon and James Braxton on a quest to find items to sell at an auction in Eastbourne. Their search takes them through East Anglia, hearing tales of a forgotten Victorian electrical pioneer who gave the world the toaster and the Essex town that changed its name to a spice that was once grown there.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge: Lose Weight for Good
programme.desc: After finding out some of the dieters have been sneaking off for takeaways, Tom decides to create his own low-calorie alternatives to fast foods and ready meals. He sets about tackling some of the most popular takeaways within the group, and creates his own nutritional version of a chicken curry, burgers, and even a doner kebab, which would typically exceed over 1,000 calories

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The 10th first-round match sees four students from Merton College, Oxford pit their wits against a team from Wolfson College, Cambridge, in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges a team of doctors to diagnose a young woman struggling with dramatic weight gain and hair loss, and another who has suddenly lost her sight in one eye. In the last few years, 26-year-old Hayley has gained 20kg and gone from a size 4-6 to a size 18-20, without changing her diet. She also struggles with muscle weakness and constant fatigue, and her hair has started falling out. While the experts begin their investigations into Hayley's complex case, Michael introduces them to another young woman who is struggling with frightening symptoms. In 2017, while on a camping holiday with her mum in the Lake District, Lotte developed a severe headache that has never stopped since, and she is now unable to see anything out of her left eye

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis look back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Milton Jones, Nish Kumar and Romesh Ranganathan. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe explores the concept of monogamy in this funny and lighthearted film, created using both live action and animation. Throughout the film, Sara discovers what makes people tick when it comes to matters of the heart, and why you should never underestimate the allure of a saxophone

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker revisits documentaries exploring the commodification of forbidden desires, taking in subjects ranging from the porn industry to the US opioid crisis. He is reunited with porn star JJ, who has turned his back on his old career to begin a new life in eastern Europe, and drug addict Katilia, who is still struggling to turn her life around

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani goes back to her Yorkshire roots exploring the lesser-known River Burn in the glorious Swinton estate. She discovers why people seem to have fallen in love with fishing since lockdown and how the humble willow is the perfect weapon against riverbank erosion. Meanwhile, Adam Henson meets this year's One Man and His Dog competitors from Northern Ireland and Wales, and John Craven joins Cerys Matthews and Simon King for the 2020 Photographic Competition

programme.title: Edinburgh Festivals - The Show Must Go On
programme.desc: Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Lolly Adefope capture the spirit of Edinburgh and bring highlights of new shows and events from the Fringe, the Book Festival and the International Festival, which are happening within the limits of the Covid-19 lockdown. Drawing on archive performances, the programme also tells the story of how the city became host to the largest, most accessible, creative arts festival in the world, while contributors including Evelyn Glennie and Fiona Shaw explain what the Festivals mean to them

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit cases previously featured to check on the progress of the investigations, beginning with the case of a wealthy female tenant poisoned by her landlord following a financial deal made shortly before her death in 1911

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson meet Esme and Richard, who live in Rugby with their seven pets and want an English country garden with areas for the animals and an outdoor cinema

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don checks the progress of the potatoes he planted earlier in the year and recommends plants for dry shade under trees. There are water-saving tips from a water scientist in Somerset who uses a variety of techniques to save as much water as possible in her own garden and advice from a gardener who has filled his home with over 1,000 houseplants. Plus, Nick Bailey on summer-flowering clematis and Ant Anderson meeting people planting up the Birmingham canal sides

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces highlights of St Helens v Hull Kingston Rovers, Leeds Rhinos v Huddersfield Giants, Hull FC v Wakefield Trinity and Castleford Tigers v Warrington Wolves

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Former legal director John Owens and property manager Julie Petford face each other in a bid to make the most profit and be crowned the winner in Stratford-upon-Avon

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Doris and Sarah break the news to Dawn's daughter Emma that her mother has died, and Roy moves into Ash Park after finding it difficult to return home

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: An examination of the vital bond between animal mothers and their babies, from the fish that looks after its young in its mouth, to the extended childhoods of gorillas and orang-utans. The programme explores the tough choices that mothers make to balance their own needs with those of their infants, and looks at how the emotions of love and grief manifest themselves in the animal kingdom. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The team go in search of the weedy sea dragon and investigate why parts of the Southern Ocean are warming much faster than other oceans

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: James Lewis and Nick Davies examine antiques brought to them at the Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, Hampshire, while Paul Martin finds out about the town's industrial heritage

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Shaun Keaveny, Rachel Parris and Jan Ravens test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Farmer head to Sedgemoor Auction in Somerset, one of the largest livestock markets in the country, to sell a selection of their finest Dexter cattle. Narrated by Bill Paterson

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedians Tim Vine and Ricky Grover search for profitable antiques in southeast Scotland, hoping they will turn a profit at Wooler in Northumberland. Tim hears the tale of a Scottish football team that inspired a generation of young men, while Ricky gets to grips with the story behind Scotland's first boxing world champion. Guiding the two funny men are David Harper and James Braxton

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The farmers prepare for the worst of the winter weather, and disaster strikes for Helen and Graham when one of their Highland cows contracts a chronic, contagious wasting disease. In the Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan prepare for a sheepdog trial, while in Cumbria, Frank and his daughter Georgia acquire a small herd of pygmy goats. Joyce travels to Edinburgh to give a speech to mark the launch of the Women in Agriculture report at the Royal Highland and Agriculture headquarters

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts and Dr Yasmin Khan investigate the excavation of a vast Georgian cemetery near Euston Station to make way for the HS2 rail link. They discover what the site reveals about the lives of the people who were buried there, from the founders of Christie's auction house to a small child who was accidentally buried alive

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: An evening of comedy at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London. Ventriloquist Nina Conti comperes the event, with a little help from her monkey, before introducing routines by stand-up stars Rob Beckett and Jimeoin

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges a team of doctors to diagnose a young woman struggling with dramatic weight gain and hair loss, and another who has suddenly lost her sight in one eye. In the last few years, 26-year-old Hayley has gained 20kg and gone from a size 4-6 to a size 18-20, without changing her diet. She also struggles with muscle weakness and constant fatigue, and her hair has started falling out. While the experts begin their investigations into Hayley's complex case, Michael introduces them to another young woman who is struggling with frightening symptoms. In 2017, while on a camping holiday with her mum in the Lake District, Lotte developed a severe headache that has never stopped since, and she is now unable to see anything out of her left eye

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: More highlights from the last two series of the motoring show, including the team taking a summer road trip in £500 convertibles, Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff taking up the brutal motorsport of Formula Off Road, and the Dallara Stradale being put through its paces on the Top Gear track

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their oldest case yet, the 1839 murder by drowning of a female passenger aboard a commercial narrow boat in Staffordshire

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the world's fastest driverless car

programme.title: Jet! When Britain Ruled the Skies
programme.desc: Two-part documentary examining advances in British aviation in the 1950s and 60s, which begins by exploring the design and technology behind a new generation of military planes including the Vulcan bomber and the Meteor jet fighter. The first programme investigates how the achievements came at a time of national austerity, and there is a tragic reminder of how some test pilots paid the ultimate price for their work. Including contributions by Norman Tebbit and Tony Blackman

programme.title: Battlefield Britain
programme.desc: The Snows use state-of-the-art graphics to re-create the Battle of Britain, one of the pivotal conflicts of the Second World War. Peter explains the role of radar in detecting Luftwaffe planes as they headed for Dover, while Dan experiences the physiological effects of the high G-forces that would have been felt by the RAF pilots

programme.title: Plague Fiction
programme.desc: In light of the Covid-19 global outbreak, Professor Laura Ashe takes a look back at the Black Death of the 14th century, the deadliest pandemic recorded in human history. Going through the literature of the time, from one of the earliest accounts in 1347, to the great Plague Diary of Samuel Pepys, Professor Ashe explores how literature helped us cope with fear and tragedy, the importance of bravery and personal sacrifice, and whether the words of the past can offer us the comfort and healing that we need now

programme.title: Birth of the British Novel
programme.desc: Author and critic Henry Hitchings explores the evolution of the novel in 18th-century Britain, and offers his view that it was a time of cultural revolution. He examines the social and political history of the period, uses paintings by great artists to illustrate scenes from key novels, and explains how major genres from light entertainment for female readers to political thrillers were perfected. Along the way, he meets authors including Martin Amis, Will Self, Tom McCarthy and Jenny Uglow

programme.title: Dancing in the Blitz: How World War Two Made British Ballet
programme.desc: Documentary in which David Bintley, director of the Birmingham Royal Ballet, explores how British ballet thrived during the war. Using rare and previously unseen archive footage, Bintley charts how the then Sadler's Wells Ballet Company, led by Ninette de Valois and featuring stars such as Margot Fonteyn and Frederick Ashton, survived an encounter with Nazi forces in the Netherlands. He reveals how they danced while the bombs fell during the Blitz to bring ballet to the public as an antidote to the austerity the nation faced - before emerging post-war as the Royal Ballet. Featuring contributions by Beryl Grey, Gillian Lynne and Julia Farron

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: A visitor to Abi's party leaves Tanya stunned, while Bianca struggles to give Morgan a birthday treat. Jean's date looks set to be a disaster, and a new cab driver is chosen

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Max can't understand Tanya's reaction to his return, but Abi's involvement soon becomes apparent. Jean becomes increasingly agitated at Fargo's, while Bianca's family cause havoc

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Max asks Tanya for a new start, but Jack has another suggestion. Jean insists on going on a date with Ted, and Dot takes drastic action to bring Stacey and Bradley together

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Max demands answers from Tanya when she reveals she's been seeing someone else. Bradley and Stacey are forced to talk about their relationship while they are locked in Dot's house

programme.title: Battlefield Britain
programme.desc: The Snows use state-of-the-art graphics to re-create the Battle of Britain, one of the pivotal conflicts of the Second World War. Peter explains the role of radar in detecting Luftwaffe planes as they headed for Dover, while Dan experiences the physiological effects of the high G-forces that would have been felt by the RAF pilots

programme.title: Canals - The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the early canal builders who struggled with the rugged terrain of England's Pennine hills. Creating a network of canals in this landscape was an major challenge, but connecting the powerhouses of Yorkshire and Lancashire was a great incentive at the time of the Industrial Revolution. Liz considers the strategic considerations and implications of this massive engineering project

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates a wetlands scene complete with warm, swampy air and overgrown trees, with hanging Spanish moss and velvety bushes

programme.title: Jet! When Britain Ruled the Skies
programme.desc: Part two of two. The development of the UK's passenger airline industry following the Second World War, which by 1960 was the global leader and boasted routes stretching throughout the Empire. The aircraft used included the British-built Vickers Viscount and de Havilland Comet - the world's first passenger jet airliner, which could fly twice as high and fast as its predecessors. Narrated by Barbara Flynn

programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. Archive footage, new film of the aircraft in flight and interviews with the last surviving veterans who flew the plane in combat combine to tell its story

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team investigates some exciting new developments in astronomy

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Planets are now being discovered outside Earth's solar system on a regular basis, and these strange new worlds are forcing scientists to rewrite the history of how the Sun and the planets, satellites, dwarf planets, asteroids and comets that orbit it came into being. This programme reveals that far from a simple story of stable objects orbiting a star, the development of the solar system was a potent combination of hellfire, chaos and planetary pinball that makes it somewhat of a miracle that Earth was created at all

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Art historian Rose Balston explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of John Constable. The artist pushed the boundaries of landscape painting, to the extent he was rejected by the establishment, but, perhaps just as surprisingly, he poured his love for one woman on to the canvas as no painter of landscapes had done before

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of how 24-year-old Tracy Edwards became the skipper of the first all-female crew to enter the Whitbread Round the World Race in 1989. The film reveals how her male competitors thought an all-women crew would never make it, while the chauvinistic yachting press took bets on her failing, and potential sponsors rejected her, fearing the crew would die at sea and generate bad publicity

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: Belfast born Gavin Doherty is known internationally for his innovative dress designs and teaching techniques. In this programme he shares the secrets of his success and introduces us to prodigy Ethan White from Carrickfergus

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates a wetlands scene complete with warm, swampy air and overgrown trees, with hanging Spanish moss and velvety bushes

programme.title: The People's War
programme.desc: Documentary marking the 75th anniversary of the official end to the Second World War. After six long years, the fighting had finally stopped. Almost everyone has a connection to those who served and in this special programme some of the BBC's News presenters have been looking at the roles their families played

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross captures the beauty of Alaska on canvas, creating a landscape with clear skies, soft cottony clouds, snow covered mountains and a breathtaking crystal lake

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The programme investigates what cats really get up to when they leave the comfort of their owners' homes. In a ground-breaking experiment, 50 domestic cats from a village in Surrey are tagged with GPS collars and cameras to record their every movement as they hunt in gardens and patrol the fences and hedgerows

programme.title: How We Tamed the Cat and Dog
programme.desc: Part two of two. Documentary examining archaeology and genetics to answer questions about cats, including where they came from and how they ended up living with humans. The programme examines how to read cats' enigmatic facial expressions, and studies the unusual behaviour of domestic pets that have turned feral

programme.title: Battle of Britain: The Real Story
programme.desc: James Holland explores the Battle of Britain from the German point of view. The author focuses on the tactics, technology and intelligence available to both nations, and examines how they decided to use their resources. He also gains access to first-hand testimonies from German pilots, and diaries and documents that reveal a previously unknown side to this confrontation

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: The story of secret agent Noor Inayat Khan, who was employed by the British in 1943. Hoping to infiltrate Nazi-occupied Paris, prime minister Winston Churchill sent her on a mission he never expected her to survive. Despite being captured, she refused to betray the Allies, even under torture

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about the world's oldest horse race, held twice a year in the Italian city of Siena since medieval times. Each of the city's 10 districts enters a rider, with deal-making and bribery as vital to success as good horsemanship. Jockey Gigi Bruschelli needs only two more victories to become the greatest champion in the event's history - but this year, he has fierce competition from his own protege

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
