programme.desc: Yabba and Timmy transform an old cardboard box into a fire engine, but end up causing more problems than they solve

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Every Friday is Baking Day in Struay and everyone creates their special treats - except Mrs McColl who is not a very good cook

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah is inspired by listening to a poetry record and decides to make up some poetry of her own

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo is excited about her new bed and can't wait to try it out. However, she's not the only one

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: W wants to be a superhero like X, but discovers saving Alphablocks is not as easy as it looks

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Learn all about the number 3 with Numberblock Three, who arrives with a bang - and a song-and-dance about her favourite number

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A three-year-old bunny meets friends and experiences emotions as he engages with the world around him

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Mayor Oki and Geoffrey make a lot of noise in an election for the next mayor of OKIDO

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies drive to the Hidey Hup in the Tubby Car and have fun getting dizzy

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: Bo's uncle gives Bo the run as he makes their evening meal

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Inside a cave the Olobobs find a sad Topsy Turvy who is the wrong way up! They create Mrs Maestro who conducts everyone in a song that rights the Topsy Turvy upside-down

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo and his friends encourage young viewers to question the world around them

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: Observational documentary on the lives of reception children at two contrasting primary schools - one rural, and one urban

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies' motorbike rider Grace shows off three machines that run on rails!

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter realises he has accidentally upset Benjamin by asking him to sit out the next garden raid. However, he finds that his cousin has gone when he goes to apologise

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Lars zooms to the rescue when Glitch and Tala go off the rails in search of a baby dragon.

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Topsy goes with Kerry and her mum to the new pottery cafe, while Mummy and Tim catch the bus to the optometrist's to collect Tim's new glasses

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie, Waffle, Ruby and Jelly have a playdate, and Mrs Hobbs has a delivery shop disaster

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: A photo of Gran Gran and JoJo's mummy when she was a little girl inspires JoJo and Gran Gran to take a walk and compare old photos of their neighbourhood to see how it has changed over time.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The friends go for a trip to the zoo after Duck says he wants to be a penguin

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Inside the little Toy House, everybody is sharing a book with pictures of tidy things

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The Hoot Planet is visiting the Clangers. But something odd has happened to the lovely music it usually plays! And then wool starts raining down from the sky

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy helps Igglepiggle search for his lost blanket, and they find it in Makka Pakka's ditch. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Angellica Bell reads No-bot: The Robot with No Bottom, by Sue Hendra

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the opening fixtures of the Premier League season, including Liverpool v Leeds United at Anfield and Fulham v Arsenal at Craven Cottage. Leeds were playing their first top-flight match since 2004, but could not have faced a sterner test against the champions, who last lost in the league at Anfield in April 2017. Meanwhile, Fulham also faced a tough challenge on their return to the league as they took on their London rivals. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by Robert Buckland MP, justice secretary, Rachel Reeves MP, shadow chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, Simon Coveney TD, Irish foreign minister and Dr Maria Van Kerkhove, WHO Technical Lead on Covid-19. The News review is with Alison Philips, editor of the Daily Mirror and Adam Fleming, BBC Newscast presenter

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales

programme.title: A Great North Run Celebration
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents a look back at highlights of through the years on the day the 40th staging of the iconic half-marathon was scheduled to take place. Jeanette Kwakye meets some of those who would have been running this year, including NHS staff, and the achievements of those who have raised money for charity are celebrated

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters explore a bungalow in the village of Chelsfield and a semi-detached house in Erith, both in the south east of London. Plus, a shop with plenty of flats above

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Mark Stacey and Gary Pe help two teams look for items at Stamford Meadows in Lincolnshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Grimsthorpe castle to find out about its royal collection

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is in Plymouth, where 400 years ago a group of Christians boarded the Mayflower for a journey across the Atlantic that changed American history. With music by Seth Lakeman and Russell Watson

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore finds a pair of seats, an Edwardian armchair and an old typewriter at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Castleford Tigers v Hull FC (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens. The match was originally scheduled for August, but had to be postponed when a number of Hull players and staff tested positive for Covid-19, but now they have recovered the team will have their eyes on the trophy they won back-to-back in 2016 and 2017. The Tigers are looking to win this trophy for the first time since 1986 and avenge a 28-12 defeat at the hands of the Airlie Birds at this stage of last season's competition

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: Three families experience the hardships of the 1920s, giving up all the modern conveniences - microwave, mobile phone, cars and fast food - for life in a mining community. Narrated by Nicola Reynolds

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are in Stoke-on-Trent to learn about an ambitious scheme to bring the countryside into the heart of the city. Margherita finds out what it takes to move a stretch of the River Trent, while Matt meets the next generation of nature lovers as he glories in mud with some of the city's youngest residents and their parents. In the light of the coronavirus pandemic, Tom Heap investigates whether the UK is sufficiently armed against further threats of diseases that spread from animals to humans, and Adam Henson meets another set of regional competitors vying to be crowned national One Man and His Dog champion

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition commemorating the 80th anniversary of the Battle of Britain and the Blitz. Combining stories from all over the country with a special event filmed at Biggin Hill Airfield, this episode tells the story of a defining moment in Britain's war, through the people and objects who were there. Fiona begins on board the iconic Supermarine Spitfire, flying over the Weald of Kent. Then, she and the team uncover the stories from family mementos, including a scramble bell that got the pilots into the air, ops room models that tracked their every move and a steering column from a British fighter shot down in combat

programme.title: Extinction: The Facts
programme.desc: With a million species at threat, David Attenborough explores the science of extinction and how this has consequences for everyone. The programme examines why biodiversity loss is happening and what it means - not just for the natural world, but for all mankind. The loss of biodiversity threatens food and water security, undermines humans' ability to control the climate, and places humans at greater risk of pandemics. Leading experts including Sir Robert Watson and plant scientist Prof Kathy Willis explain how all groups in the natural world are in decline, and that extinction is now happening up to 100 times faster than the natural evolutionary rate

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Robin meets Raphael for a drink, and he explains more about Freddie's bullying of Rhiannon, which gives Geraint a strong motive for Chiswell's murder. They are interrupted by a call from Matthew, which Robin blocks before revealing that they have separated. The police show Robin and Strike CCTV footage of Kinvara at Paddington Station the night before Chiswell died, while Tegan reveals that she previously witnessed Kinvara hit Chiswell over the head with a hammer. Crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling that chronicle cases investigated by private detective Cormoran Strike. Starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Micah Richards to review the latest Premier League fixtures on the opening weekend of the season, featuring West Bromwich Albion v Leicester City at the Hawthorns and Tottenham Hotspur v Everton at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: After losing their opening two games of the season, West Ham United's women's team are preparing to take on their bogey team Manchester City, who are packed full of England superstars. Within their own team, club captain Gilly Flaherty starts thinking about what life might be like when her football career comes to an end, French superstar Kenza Dali opens up about her mother's illness, and there is a rare appearance on the bench for academy player Victoria - who is still working hard on her GCSE exams. Narrated by Jaime Winstone

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters all explore mid-terraces as Martin Roberts visits Burton upon Trent, Dion Dublin takes a trip to Merseyside and Martel Maxwell is in Bromley, Kent

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Members of the England Illegal Money Lending team launch an early-morning operation against suspected loan sharks, and share one of the strangest places they have found hidden cash

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special show to commemorate the 80th anniversary of the Battle of Britain. Charlie Ross visits the RAF Museum and pays his respects to pilots at the Battle of Britain Memorial

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama set during the Second World War, following the lives and loves of women who decide to serve their country by joining the Women's Land Army. New girl Connie Carter causes mayhem from the moment she arrives, Finch desperately tries to save his beloved pet pig, and a visit from a wealthy American industrialist spells intrigue at the Manor House. Starring Becci Gemmell, Jo Woodcock and Selene Hizli

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore what Britons ate during wartime and discover how some of the UK's favourite dishes have evolved from rationing. The duo also meet army cooks to find out what troops on the front line eat today, and prepare rabbit stew and carrot cake

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a sociable couple find a country home in Bedfordshire with enough room for them, their dogs and their very own bespoke garden pub, on a £500,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson meet Esme and Richard, who live in Rugby with their seven pets and want an English country garden with areas for the animals and an outdoor cinema

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, tackling a wooden trunk, a memorial trophy and a gyroscopic toy cyclist

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Investigating one of the world's most brutal trades - the buying and selling of human organs. The programme meets the African migrants who have been exploited for their body parts by criminal gangs. Some have agreed to sell a kidney to finance their journey to Europe, only to be ripped off by the traffickers after the operation. Other victims have their organs taken without consent. Panorama also tracks down the criminals running the trade to find out how they arrange illegal operations in hospitals and clinics

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle packs as much as she can, having told Gray she is going on a last-minute holiday with her family - but he remains suspicious. Tina and Frankie grow closer when they go for a drink at the Vic after work. But why is Frankie so interested in the Carters? Max is happy his divorce from Rainie has finally come through and thanks Ian for helping him out, telling him to keep £2,000 of the money he owes him. The rest, however, he wants back now. Ben takes Callum's advice and asks Phil what to do about the situation with Danny

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the reality of life in Britain's biggest special school. In the first episode pupils face their fears at an outdoor adventure centre. The focus of the trip is for the young people to have an opportunity to challenge their disability physically, and abseiling in a wheelchair epitomises this experience

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: One-off edition of the observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. The programmes examines how long-serving and newly-qualified Northumbria Police officers respond to the changing world of modern crime, including managing online sex offenders and the threat of firearms. In one of Northumbria's newest departments - the Paedophile Online Investigation Team - Detective Constable Dave Kernaghan is managing a huge rise in new cases, but as he continues to investigate the escalating number of jobs, the work itself takes a toll as he assesses the impact this is having on his own life

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Idris Elba proves he is a dab hand at everything, Scribbler P returns for a lesson on blackface, and Mozart invents a new hit

programme.title: Yung Filly's Celeb Lock-In
programme.desc: Celebrity game show presented by the viral comedian. Featuring prank calls, exclusive house tours and lots of chaos. In this first episode, Filly is joined by EastEnders star Maisie Smith and her mum Julia. Contains some strong language

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with broadcaster and journalist Emma Barnett and stand-up Tom Allen joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team assess properties about to be auctioned in Caerphilly in Wales, Bootle on Merseyside, and Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in Belfast show off a collection of designer goods they seized from a suspected paramilitary drug dealer, and a man who stole from his girlfriend has his assets sold off

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright reports on a Liverpool street with an infestation of vermin and a rented property above a row of shops that housing officers consider unsafe for habitation

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams competing in Builth Wells, Powys. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors proceedings and also learns about Wales' washing machine industry

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Bea falls for an Italian PoW, and Connie flirts with a bashful piano player while entertaining the troops. Meanwhile, Martin discovers he has detached retinas following his accident, and Lady Hoxley's sister Diana snags herself a wealthy American admirer while helping Jack search for his grandson. Second World War drama, starring Jo Woodcock

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore Britain's fondness for picnics, preparing an alfresco lunch at Beaufort Polo Club in the Cotswolds. They also discover how the habit of outdoor dining in summer evolved into its current form, celebrate snacks such as Cornish pasties and Scotch eggs, and bake a Dundee cake

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple living in London's Docklands to find a home with some green space in Buckinghamshire's Chiltern Hills, and pays a visit to the Henley Regatta

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team tries to transform a Reading garden to make it safe for children - despite the fact that no landscaper has been willing to take on the broken slate and rotten decking

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Experts restore a tarnished rugby trophy, a hand-operated Victorian sewing machine, and a 1930s accordion, which has 64 individual components in need of repair

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: At the seaside, Mitch realises something is wrong with Chantelle, but rather than open up to her dad, she simply tells him she is missing the kids. Determined to help, he heads back to Walford to fetch them - but his plan soon backfires. Meanwhile, Ian makes a bid to become chairman of the council planning committee, and Ben fills Jay in on his plan to set up Danny

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Manchester single mothers Hayley and Megan join forces to help improve each other's diet, and turn to Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin for help in finding healthy meals all of their children will enjoy. Chris also visits a butcher to report on the importance of buying locally produced meat and asks members of the public what they think of some of the lesser known, cheaper cuts

programme.title: The Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Actor Ewan McGregor and his brother Colin, an RAF pilot, retell the story of the 1940 air battle that proved to be a turning point in the Second World War. The pair get hands-on experience of flying period fighter aircraft across the skies of England, giving Colin an opportunity to contrast his training in modern combat techniques with the skills required to master the Spitfire's less sophisticated technology. The duo also meet some of those who participated in the conflict itself

programme.title: Hell on Wheels
programme.desc: Changes to the team line-up have a devastating effect on morale, as defensive players who have learned to work well together find themselves split up. After 10 years of roller derby, team captain Kid is starting to think its time to hang up her skates, but emotions run high when she makes a surprise announcement after a victory against Birmingham

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Three more patients undergo treatment for skin conditions with the help of leading medical experts. Max was left with a scab on top of his head caused by sunburn six years ago, Eva suffers from a condition that causes excessive sweating, and Alix cannot bear to look at herself in the mirror because of her acne

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: The final batch of patients undergoing life-changing treatment for skin conditions share their stories. Hannah has had a severe case of eczema since she was two years old, Esiri suffers with hyperpigmentation that over-the-counter products have done nothing for, and Scot has become increasingly sensitive about his psoriasis over the course of his life

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a mortgage advisor, a mother and daughter, and a print manager

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the opening fixtures of the Premier League season, including Liverpool v Leeds United at Anfield and Fulham v Arsenal at Craven Cottage. programme.desc: A round-up of political news and events at Westminster and Holyrood.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson assists a couple who have a £500,000 budget and a dream of leaving their city life in Bristol behind them and finding their perfect country home in Monmouthshire. While in the county, keen archaeologist Jules visits the village of Trellech, where local excavation efforts are unearthing what could be the largest medieval settlement in Wales.

programme.desc: Members of the England Illegal Money Lending team launch an early-morning operation against suspected loan sharks, and share one of the strangest places they have found hidden cash

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets a Merseyside tenant in poor health who is trapped in a damp rented property, while a Birmingham resident has vanished into thin air - leaving their home empty

programme.title: Politics Scotland
programme.desc: Live coverage of today's briefing from the Scottish Government on the Coronavirus outbreak

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama set during the Second World War, following the lives and loves of women who decide to serve their country by joining the Women's Land Army. New girl Connie Carter causes mayhem from the moment she arrives, Finch desperately tries to save his beloved pet pig, and a visit from a wealthy American industrialist spells intrigue at the Manor House. Starring Becci Gemmell, Jo Woodcock and Selene Hizli

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore what Britons ate during wartime and discover how some of the UK's favourite dishes have evolved from rationing. The duo also meet army cooks to find out what troops on the front line eat today, and prepare rabbit stew and carrot cake

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a sociable couple find a country home in Bedfordshire with enough room for them, their dogs and their very own bespoke garden pub, on a £500,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson meet Esme and Richard, who live in Rugby with their seven pets and want an English country garden with areas for the animals and an outdoor cinema

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, tackling a wooden trunk, a memorial trophy and a gyroscopic toy cyclist

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Laura Miller

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Investigating one of the world's most brutal trades - the buying and selling of human organs. The programme meets the African migrants who have been exploited for their body parts by criminal gangs. Some have agreed to sell a kidney to finance their journey to Europe, only to be ripped off by the traffickers after the operation. Other victims have their organs taken without consent. Panorama also tracks down the criminals running the trade to find out how they arrange illegal operations in hospitals and clinics

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle packs as much as she can, having told Gray she is going on a last-minute holiday with her family - but he remains suspicious. Tina and Frankie grow closer when they go for a drink at the Vic after work. But why is Frankie so interested in the Carters? Max is happy his divorce from Rainie has finally come through and thanks Ian for helping him out, telling him to keep £2,000 of the money he owes him. The rest, however, he wants back now. Ben takes Callum's advice and asks Phil what to do about the situation with Danny

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by actress and presenter Mel Giedroyc, heavyweight boxer David Haye, actor-musician Martin Kemp and comedian Romesh Ranganathan. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory and the dubious honour of being dubbed host Rob Brydon's liar of the week

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: One-off edition of the observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. The programmes examines how long-serving and newly-qualified Northumbria Police officers respond to the changing world of modern crime, including managing online sex offenders and the threat of firearms. In one of Northumbria's newest departments - the Paedophile Online Investigation Team - Detective Constable Dave Kernaghan is managing a huge rise in new cases, but as he continues to investigate the escalating number of jobs, the work itself takes a toll as he assesses the impact this is having on his own life

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Idris Elba proves he is a dab hand at everything, Scribbler P returns for a lesson on blackface, and Mozart invents a new hit

programme.title: Yung Filly's Celeb Lock-In
programme.desc: Celebrity game show presented by the viral comedian. Featuring prank calls, exclusive house tours and lots of chaos. In this first episode, Filly is joined by EastEnders star Maisie Smith and her mum Julia. Contains some strong language

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with broadcaster and journalist Emma Barnett and stand-up Tom Allen joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team assess properties about to be auctioned in Caerphilly in Wales, Bootle on Merseyside, and Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in Belfast show off a collection of designer goods they seized from a suspected paramilitary drug dealer, and a man who stole from his girlfriend has his assets sold off

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright reports on a Liverpool street with an infestation of vermin and a rented property above a row of shops that housing officers consider unsafe for habitation

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams competing in Builth Wells, Powys. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors proceedings and also learns about Wales' washing machine industry

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Bea falls for an Italian PoW, and Connie flirts with a bashful piano player while entertaining the troops. Meanwhile, Martin discovers he has detached retinas following his accident, and Lady Hoxley's sister Diana snags herself a wealthy American admirer while helping Jack search for his grandson. Second World War drama, starring Jo Woodcock

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore Britain's fondness for picnics, preparing an alfresco lunch at Beaufort Polo Club in the Cotswolds. They also discover how the habit of outdoor dining in summer evolved into its current form, celebrate snacks such as Cornish pasties and Scotch eggs, and bake a Dundee cake

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple living in London's Docklands to find a home with some green space in Buckinghamshire's Chiltern Hills, and pays a visit to the Henley Regatta

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team tries to transform a Reading garden to make it safe for children - despite the fact that no landscaper has been willing to take on the broken slate and rotten decking

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Experts restore a tarnished rugby trophy, a hand-operated Victorian sewing machine, and a 1930s accordion, which has 64 individual components in need of repair

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: At the seaside, Mitch realises something is wrong with Chantelle, but rather than open up to her dad, she simply tells him she is missing the kids. Determined to help, he heads back to Walford to fetch them - but his plan soon backfires. Meanwhile, Ian makes a bid to become chairman of the council planning committee, and Ben fills Jay in on his plan to set up Danny

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Manchester single mothers Hayley and Megan join forces to help improve each other's diet, and turn to Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin for help in finding healthy meals all of their children will enjoy. Chris also visits a butcher to report on the importance of buying locally produced meat and asks members of the public what they think of some of the lesser known, cheaper cuts

programme.title: The Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Actor Ewan McGregor and his brother Colin, an RAF pilot, retell the story of the 1940 air battle that proved to be a turning point in the Second World War. The pair get hands-on experience of flying period fighter aircraft across the skies of England, giving Colin an opportunity to contrast his training in modern combat techniques with the skills required to master the Spitfire's less sophisticated technology. The duo also meet some of those who participated in the conflict itself

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Three more patients undergo treatment for skin conditions with the help of leading medical experts. Max was left with a scab on top of his head caused by sunburn six years ago, Eva suffers from a condition that causes excessive sweating, and Alix cannot bear to look at herself in the mirror because of her acne

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: The final batch of patients undergoing life-changing treatment for skin conditions share their stories. Hannah has had a severe case of eczema since she was two years old, Esiri suffers with hyperpigmentation that over-the-counter products have done nothing for, and Scot has become increasingly sensitive about his psoriasis over the course of his life

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a mortgage advisor, a mother and daughter, and a print manager

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the opening fixtures of the Premier League season, including Liverpool v Leeds United at Anfield and Fulham v Arsenal at Craven Cottage. Leeds were playing their first top-flight match since 2004, but could not have faced a sterner test against the champions, who last lost in the league at Anfield in April 2017. Meanwhile, Fulham also faced a tough challenge on their return to the league as they took on their London rivals. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by Robert Buckland MP, justice secretary, Rachel Reeves MP, shadow chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, Simon Coveney TD, Irish foreign minister and Dr Maria Van Kerkhove, WHO Technical Lead on Covid-19. The News review is with Alison Philips, editor of the Daily Mirror and Adam Fleming, BBC Newscast presenter

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: The political developments of the week, with policy-makers questioned on the key issues

programme.title: A Great North Run Celebration
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents a look back at highlights of through the years on the day the 40th staging of the iconic half-marathon was scheduled to take place. Jeanette Kwakye meets some of those who would have been running this year, including NHS staff, and the achievements of those who have raised money for charity are celebrated

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters explore a bungalow in the village of Chelsfield and a semi-detached house in Erith, both in the south east of London. Plus, a shop with plenty of flats above

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Mark Stacey and Gary Pe help two teams look for items at Stamford Meadows in Lincolnshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Grimsthorpe castle to find out about its royal collection

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is in Plymouth, where 400 years ago a group of Christians boarded the Mayflower for a journey across the Atlantic that changed American history. With music by Seth Lakeman and Russell Watson

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore finds a pair of seats, an Edwardian armchair and an old typewriter at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Castleford Tigers v Hull FC (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens. The match was originally scheduled for August, but had to be postponed when a number of Hull players and staff tested positive for Covid-19, but now they have recovered the team will have their eyes on the trophy they won back-to-back in 2016 and 2017. The Tigers are looking to win this trophy for the first time since 1986 and avenge a 28-12 defeat at the hands of the Airlie Birds at this stage of last season's competition

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson assists a couple who have a £500,000 budget and a dream of leaving their city life in Bristol behind them and finding their perfect country home in Monmouthshire. While in the county, keen archaeologist Jules visits the village of Trellech, where local excavation efforts are unearthing what could be the largest medieval settlement in Wales.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are in Stoke-on-Trent to learn about an ambitious scheme to bring the countryside into the heart of the city. Margherita finds out what it takes to move a stretch of the River Trent, while Matt meets the next generation of nature lovers as he glories in mud with some of the city's youngest residents and their parents. In the light of the coronavirus pandemic, Tom Heap investigates whether the UK is sufficiently armed against further threats of diseases that spread from animals to humans, and Adam Henson meets another set of regional competitors vying to be crowned national One Man and His Dog champion

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition commemorating the 80th anniversary of the Battle of Britain and the Blitz. Combining stories from all over the country with a special event filmed at Biggin Hill Airfield, this episode tells the story of a defining moment in Britain's war, through the people and objects who were there. Fiona begins on board the iconic Supermarine Spitfire, flying over the Weald of Kent. Then, she and the team uncover the stories from family mementos, including a scramble bell that got the pilots into the air, ops room models that tracked their every move and a steering column from a British fighter shot down in combat

programme.title: Extinction: The Facts
programme.desc: With a million species at threat, David Attenborough explores the science of extinction and how this has consequences for everyone. The programme examines why biodiversity loss is happening and what it means - not just for the natural world, but for all mankind. The loss of biodiversity threatens food and water security, undermines humans' ability to control the climate, and places humans at greater risk of pandemics. Leading experts including Sir Robert Watson and plant scientist Prof Kathy Willis explain how all groups in the natural world are in decline, and that extinction is now happening up to 100 times faster than the natural evolutionary rate

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Robin meets Raphael for a drink, and he explains more about Freddie's bullying of Rhiannon, which gives Geraint a strong motive for Chiswell's murder. They are interrupted by a call from Matthew, which Robin blocks before revealing that they have separated. The police show Robin and Strike CCTV footage of Kinvara at Paddington Station the night before Chiswell died, while Tegan reveals that she previously witnessed Kinvara hit Chiswell over the head with a hammer. Crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling that chronicle cases investigated by private detective Cormoran Strike. Starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Micah Richards to review the latest Premier League fixtures on the opening weekend of the season, featuring West Bromwich Albion v Leicester City at the Hawthorns and Tottenham Hotspur v Everton at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: After losing their opening two games of the season, West Ham United's women's team are preparing to take on their bogey team Manchester City, who are packed full of England superstars. Within their own team, club captain Gilly Flaherty starts thinking about what life might be like when her football career comes to an end, French superstar Kenza Dali opens up about her mother's illness, and there is a rare appearance on the bench for academy player Victoria - who is still working hard on her GCSE exams. Narrated by Jaime Winstone

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.desc: A celebration of Northern Irish weddings from the past 60 years. Featuring contributions from couples of various generations who look back on their special day and the cultural influences they were inspired to emulate - from Princess Diana's record-breaking train to the Beatles' famous haircuts

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: One-off edition of the observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: The ex-footballer reflects on his upbringing in Belfast's Shankill district and his career at the top of his sport during the 1980s. Raised in Belfast's Shankill district, Norman went on to become the youngest player to score for Manchester United's first team, the youngest FA Cup scorer in history and the youngest ever footballer to play in World Cup finals. However, within a decade, his career was cut cruelly short. Speaking to Stephen Watson, Norman reveals the true story behind his sporting life - what it was like to have and then lose it all - and how he managed to rebuild his life. Featuring unseen archive material

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: UK Prime Minister Boris Johnson faces momentous challenges. The coronavirus pandemic, an economic slump and a looming moment of truth for Britain's relations with the EU. In the midst of this turbulence the future of the United Kingdom itself looks uncertain. Polls suggest increasing numbers of Scots want out of the Union. HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the new leader of the Scottish Conservative party, Douglas Ross. Are events playing into the hands of the Scottish nationalists?

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Investigating one of the world's most brutal trades - the buying and selling of human organs. The programme meets the African migrants who have been exploited for their body parts by criminal gangs. Some have agreed to sell a kidney to finance their journey to Europe, only to be ripped off by the traffickers after the operation. Other victims have their organs taken without consent. Panorama also tracks down the criminals running the trade to find out how they arrange illegal operations in hospitals and clinics

programme.desc: One of the most important texts in the Scottish Gaelic language, the Book of Deer was written in the ancient monastery of Deer that disappeared over a thousand years ago. This is the story of a team of archaeologists and volunteers who spent nine days hunting for the lost monastery of Deer, where the book was written. In the event, those involved found more than they imagined possible

programme.desc: On a very hot day, Mike invites Iris to join him for a cooling cocktail on the terrace, but disaster strikes when the raccoons steal the ice maker.

programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Billy Ocean, Frankie Bridge and Russell Howard join Martin and Roman Kemp for the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Wildlife presenter Ben Fogle and actress Phyllis Logan join the presenter in his celebration of the great British countryside. Alan also meets a master willow-basket weaver, looks back to the flower trends of the 1980s and samples British sparkling wine with Clodagh McKenna. Plus, more from the organic halal farming family the Radwans

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford visit the country's most fascinating attractions. This week they are at the Black Country Living Museum in the West Midlands, where they drive vintage vehicles and learn the traditional skills of canal boat art and chain-making

programme.title: Love Your Garden Themed Specials
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh looks back on memorable family gardens created in the series, celebrating the unusual twists the team have put on creating the perfect environment for children. The programme focuses on three episodes in particular, in which the experts created a hangout for teenagers, a woodland adventure garden, and a fairy-tale glade

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott recreates some of his mother's recipes, including brown chicken stew and rice n' peas. Country music duo the Shires take over the kitchen for a jam session and a late night pasta feast, while Juliet Sear shares her mother's secret quiche recipe and Ainsley knocks up some homemade garlic bread and coconut and lime fairy cakes

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the second semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Ciro, Paiton, Simon and Anne work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature, alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as comedian Paul Merton, actress Sally Lindsay and Made in Chelsea's Jamie Laing take part in the celebrity version of the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Captain Sir Tom Moore joins Piers Morgan to look back on his extraordinary life - which spans an entire century. He reflects on the devastating accident that left him in intensive care at the age of 98 - and how his gratitude to the NHS and battle to regain mobility led to the record-breaking charity walk which first brought him to public attention. Tom takes stock of his wartime service, reveals the untold story of his first marriage and discusses how his beloved second wife Pamela brought him happiness and a family before her terminal illness left him rebuilding his life once again

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: For rubber baron Walter Blackett and his family, life in 1941 Singapore is full of wealth and privilege, where the horrors of war-torn Europe are a distant whisper. But when their power and stability are rocked by new arrivals and the looming threat of a Japanese invasion, Walter has a cunning plan. Adaptation of the novel by Booker Prize-winner JG Farrell, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Charles Dance, Jane Horrocks and Colm Meaney

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams presents a further compilation of TV clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. Clips include Olly Murs being attacked by a fly on The Voice, a goat running amok in Judge Rinder's court, a Love islander discovering the danger of wearing high heels on TV, and a desperate dog relieving himself on Paul O'Grady

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Staff at the airport deal with one of the UK's worst winter storms, with winds raging out on the airfield. Meanwhile, rookie passenger experience manager Lee finds himself dealing with his first emergency fire

Nurse Charlotte has a particularly unusual patient to treat as a stray corn snake is brought in, while some cute kittens visit the mobile unit for vaccinations

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Asha sets out to clear the air with Dev, Oliver's doctor delivers a devastating verdict and David grows wary of Ray's motives

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Fact-based drama starring David Tennant as notorious serial killer Dennis Nilsen. On 9 February 1983, Detective Chief Inspector Peter Jay is called to the leafy north London address of 23 Cranley Gardens, where he discovers the drains clogged with rotting human flesh and bones. But the extent of this grisly discovery only becomes apparent when the culprit returns from work. Dennis Nilsen, a meek man, freely admits to the police that it's not just one or two bodies, but as many as 15 or 16. As the police try to secure a conviction and identify the victims from scant DNA evidence, biographer Brian Masters attempts to understand why. A game of chess with 'Des' begins, and while Brian may be exhilarated by his newest subject, he's unaware what this new obsession could do to his life. Also starring Daniel Mays, Jason Watkins and Barry Ward

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is thrust into turmoil, Dawn and Harriet are unsettled and Dan struggles to pay his bills

programme.title: Harbour Cops
programme.desc: Documentary following the daily work of Dyfed-Powys Police officers over the busy summer months. Dyfed-Powys is the smallest police force in the UK, but has to patrol the largest police area, their jurisdiction taking in national parks and a lengthy run of the spectacular South Wales coastline. In this first episode, officers in the seaside town of Tenby on the western side of Carmarthen Bay have to deal with rowdy holidaymakers causing trouble and track down a pensioner who has been reported missing

programme.title: Flying for Britain with David Jason
programme.desc: The actor meets members of the RAF's Battle of Britain Memorial Flight, charged with preserving historic aircraft from the Second World War. The team is emerging from lockdown, having been forced to cancel several anniversary events. A qualified pilot, David joins them in their historic Spitfires and Hurricanes for a special tribute fly-past

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Des withdraws his cooperation from the police just as Jay thinks they have what they need to convict him of all the murders. Instead, he begins confiding in Brian, leaving Jay faced with the challenge of having to deal with a criminal biographer for the first time in his career. Des sessions with Brian give him the chance to analyse his own behaviour, leading him to make the shocking decision to plead not guilty in court

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the royals' rollercoaster relationship with the press, beginning with the Queen's 1953-1954 Commonwealth tour, when cameramen worked hand-in-hand with the palace. A few years later, newspaper owner Lord Altrincham was attacked for daring to publish criticisms of the Queen, but by the mid-1970s Princess Margaret's hedonistic lifestyle was the subject of increasing press attention. But that was only the beginning. In the 1990s, the press has a field day over the breakdown of Charles and Diana's marriage, feeding on royal scandals

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Des withdraws his cooperation from the police just as Jay thinks they have what they need to convict him of all the murders. Instead, he begins confiding in Brian, leaving Jay faced with the challenge of having to deal with a criminal biographer for the first time in his career. Des sessions with Brian give him the chance to analyse his own behaviour, leading him to make the shocking decision to plead not guilty in court

programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel shoot people with toy arrows to make them fall in love with Fizz. But Magoo mistakes them for a giant hornet.

programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers compete in a series of challenges to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Katie, Les, Justin and Jane pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is thrust into turmoil, Dawn and Harriet are unsettled and Dan struggles to pay his bills

programme.title: Harbour Cops
programme.desc: Documentary following the daily work of Dyfed-Powys Police officers over the busy summer months. Dyfed-Powys is the smallest police force in the UK, but has to patrol the largest police area, their jurisdiction taking in national parks and a lengthy run of the spectacular South Wales coastline. In this first episode, officers in the seaside town of Tenby on the western side of Carmarthen Bay have to deal with rowdy holidaymakers causing trouble and track down a pensioner who has been reported missing

programme.title: Flying for Britain with David Jason
programme.desc: The actor meets members of the RAF's Battle of Britain Memorial Flight, charged with preserving historic aircraft from the Second World War. The team is emerging from lockdown, having been forced to cancel several anniversary events. A qualified pilot, David joins them in their historic Spitfires and Hurricanes for a special tribute fly-past

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Des withdraws his cooperation from the police just as Jay thinks they have what they need to convict him of all the murders. Instead, he begins confiding in Brian, leaving Jay faced with the challenge of having to deal with a criminal biographer for the first time in his career. Des sessions with Brian give him the chance to analyse his own behaviour, leading him to make the shocking decision to plead not guilty in court

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the royals' rollercoaster relationship with the press, beginning with the Queen's 1953-1954 Commonwealth tour, when cameramen worked hand-in-hand with the palace. A few years later, newspaper owner Lord Altrincham was attacked for daring to publish criticisms of the Queen, but by the mid-1970s Princess Margaret's hedonistic lifestyle was the subject of increasing press attention. But that was only the beginning. In the 1990s, the press has a field day over the breakdown of Charles and Diana's marriage, feeding on royal scandals

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Des withdraws his cooperation from the police just as Jay thinks they have what they need to convict him of all the murders. Instead, he begins confiding in Brian, leaving Jay faced with the challenge of having to deal with a criminal biographer for the first time in his career. Des sessions with Brian give him the chance to analyse his own behaviour, leading him to make the shocking decision to plead not guilty in court

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: The key moments from stage 16, featuring a mountainous 164km course from La Tour-du-Pin to Villard-de-Lans. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Another chance to see the feature-length first episode of the popular detective drama. Inspector Jack Frost's investigation into the case of a missing child uncovers a skeleton chained to a locked strongbox that tantalisingly turns out to be empty, but provides evidence of a crime committed 30 years previously. David Jason stars as the irascible detective, with Bruce Alexander, Matt Bardock and Claire Hackett

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Rishi Persad introduces live coverage of five races from the Curragh, along with three races from Chelmsford. From the Curragh: The 2.35 Blandford Stakes, 3.10 Flying Five, 3.40 Moyglare Stakes, 4.10 Vincent O'Brien National Stakes and 4.40 Irish St Leger. With analysis from Matt Chapman, Sally Ann Grassick and Kevin Blake, reports from Natalie Green and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The headmaster of a local school is murdered, his body found pinned to the wall in the manner of his own butterfly collection. The dead man was a mysterious international club for geniuses, which are currently testing the school's pupils for possible new recruits. Barnaby and Winter investigate the secretive organisation, which has proven controversial in the local area, but also uncover the possibility of a connection between the killing and the impact of pesticides on a rare species of butterfly

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the royals' rollercoaster relationship with the press, beginning with the Queen's 1953-1954 Commonwealth tour, when cameramen worked hand-in-hand with the palace. A few years later, newspaper owner Lord Altrincham was attacked for daring to publish criticisms of the Queen, but by the mid-1970s Princess Margaret's hedonistic lifestyle was the subject of increasing press attention. But that was only the beginning. In the 1990s, the press has a field day over the breakdown of Charles and Diana's marriage, feeding on royal scandals

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: It's the final visit to the Sunday night period drama, giving viewers a chance to find out how writer Andrew Davies has brought his adaptation of Jane Austen's unfinished novel to an end. On the night of the Midsummer Ball, romantic interests are finally resolved. However, the fate of Sanditon is in the balance when events conspire to leave Charlotte and the Parker family facing down impossible choices. Rose Williams stars as the feisty heroine, with Theo James, Anne Reid and Kris Marshall

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Tom McMahon becomes the third victim of a spate of suspicious fires, Banks realises he is now on the trail of a serial killer. A new discovery about the methods used by the murderer casts doubt on the alibis of all his suspects and the detective is determined to get to the truth at the Asperns' house - despite being warned by his superiors to stay away from the family. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Cabbot investigates when a woman is found murdered on the moors. As the identity of the victim is established, it becomes apparent any number of suspects may have had a reason to want her dead. Meanwhile, Banks is called in when the body of a teenage girl is discovered in a storage room down an alleyway. Stephen Tompkinson and Andrea Lowe star

programme.title: Davina McCall: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: Davina travels to the Norwegian archipelago of Svalbard in the Arctic Circle, a harsh environment set across 15 million sq km of sea ice where temperatures can drop to -40C. She learns how huskies' thick fur is better insulation than goose down, that the deep pads on their paws protect them from the cold and their almond shaped eyes mean they are less exposed in a blizzard. She also tracks a polar bear to the open sea and witnesses bearded seals using their whiskers like feelers to find shrimp and crab on the ocean floor

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul and the staff prepare for a visit from the Queen, who is arriving to officially open the new kennels, and one lucky dog is chosen for a special audience with the royal visitor - corgi Beama, who is paying her second visit to Battersea in 12 years. Elsewhere, the rehoming van has travelled all the way to the Isle of Wight to pick up eight Chihuahua siblings, whose owner sadly had no choice but to give them up, and handler James helps four-year-old pug Maggie comes to terms with being blind

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Former nanny Lavinia Seagram is found soaked in blood and it is believed she had turned to drink and drugs to commit suicide. However, Poirot is soon on the scent of troubled heiress Norma Restarick, who had problems of her own - not least confessing to the possibility she has killed someone herself. Guest starring Jemima Rooper, Zoe Wanamaker and Peter Bowles, with David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. As plans to stage a celebratory re-enactment of a Victorian prizefight shape up, jealousy, betrayal and blackmail take a ringside seat, while two suspicious deaths suggest Barnaby has let friendship cloud his judgement. Detective drama, guest starring Phil Daniels (Quadrophenia, EastEnders), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A casino is robbed, presenting Frost with a fresh challenge to his deductive powers, and things go from bad to worse when a local copper is reported missing and a well-known armed thug is spotted in Denton. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and George Anton

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones sets out to explore eight very disparate kinds of quintessentially British terrain, investigating how they have dictated different ways of living, working and playing - both in the past and as they are now - across the entire country, while also unearthing quirky and funny facts en route. He begins by heading to the South Downs, which are situated in Hampshire and East and West Sussex, taking to the air for a bird's eye view and attempting to get to know one of the region's woolliest inhabitants

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the picturesque estuaries of Suffolk and Essex in the second leg of his journey through the British landscape. Amongst his challenges are attempting to eat a 'Colchester Native', helping to build a replica of the Mayflower and enjoying the beauty of Constable country

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective and his team find themselves investigating the murder of one of their own. Cabbot is convinced there is a link between the deaths of Lucy Payne and her colleague, but is dismissed as trying to push her career ambitions. However, after receiving new information from another force, she begins to uncover the real connection between the killings. Andrea Lowe stars

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Recently single teacher Laura Nielson is set up with Andrew Earlham, a widowed surgeon and the father of one of her students. The two enjoy a first date, but the next day rumours and accusations begin to circulate and the two find themselves entangled in a web of deceit and confusion. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd, with Shelley Conn, Zoe Tapper and Warren Brown

programme.title: A Touch of Frost

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A planning dispute leads to murder after a stalwart in the Midsomer Conservation Society suspects her neighbours are involved in a money-making development scam. Political wrangling, burglaries and sexual liaisons abound in picturesque Great Pelfe as Barnaby and Jones investigate. Guest starring Hugo Speer (Bleak House), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prestigious golf club is the setting for murder when a player is bludgeoned to death on the 13th hole. Barnaby and Jones discover gambling, illegal money-lending and assault are rife among the snobbish members, who are also dismissive of the local villagers and only allow them on the course for a few hours a week. Before long, another body turns up, making the detectives doubly determined to catch this terror of the fairways. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Nicholas Le Prevost, John Standing and real-life golfer Sam Torrance

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective is drawn into the criminal underworld of Soho when he reluctantly accepts an unofficial request from Chief Superintendent Rydell to find his missing daughter. Meanwhile, an armed robbery in Eastvale turns into a murder investigation after the perpetrator is found dead in his home and when a battered body is found in a local nightclub, the identity of the victim leads Banks and Cabbot to a devastating realisation about both cases

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura's online attack on Andrew yields unexpected consequences as a figure from her past returns to haunt her, potentially jeopardising the case. Laura resorts to extreme measures to find hard evidence of Andrew's guilt

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars
