programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enlists the help of paper-cutting expert Poppy Chancellor to make personalised wedding invitations for a couple about to tie the knot. Plus, the inventive presenter creates a craft collage to celebrate the arrival of a newborn baby

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Gloucestershire couple Helen and Charles Taverner are looking for a holiday home on Lake Como that they can spend some money, time and love on. Sara Damergi leaves no stone unturned showing the couple how far their budget of £50,000 will stretch in this desirable part of Italy

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole is rather taken with a throne salvaged from an exotic nightclub and Simon O'Brien's restoration of a Victorian doll's house fails to go to plan

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Northamptonshire and Coventry, where Henry discovers a rare limited edition Honda Goldwing motorbike with only 65 miles on the clock

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie prepares to end the year in style by throwing a Christmas dinner to remember. However, with the ancient range cooker out of action and the 19th-century bread oven put into service cooking the turkey, it's a tall order to deliver a five-course extravaganza for 17 guests. Angel sets Dick the task of finding and chopping the perfect logs for her Christmas table display in their 12-acre grounds. Christmas cheer threatens to run dry for south Londoners Lee and Belinda as freezing temperatures and a broken boiler put their first ever festive event in peril

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets retired couple Steve and Chris Elliot, who are building an Arts and Crafts-inspired modern eco-home. DIY enthusiast Steve wants to do a lot of the work himself, but the presenter warns against it as it could take up too much time, and the pair want to be moved in within a year

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Old Stables, Somerset, where Finnish-born Nina Dodd and her husband Andy welcome guests to their Scandinavian-style accommodation. While some are impressed by the immaculate interior, David and Erica Hensley are not pleased about the no-shoes policy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Rigsby's Guest House in Hertfordshire, where owner Morris Cockman entertains his rivals with a visit to a local zoo. Not all of his guests are impressed, and a slow breakfast service does not improve his chances of success

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David and Erica Hensley host at the Fairway in Blackpool, where they hope to win over their guests with a spot of classic seaside entertainment. After taking their fellow contestants on a horror walk, the couple will be wary of any unexpected surprises when it comes to their scores

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Robin Hughes is the final host of the week in the converted Dent Station in the Yorkshire Dales. He organises a butchery competition and high-end food, before some home truths about the group dynamic are revealed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners meet one last time to discover how much their rivals thought they should receive and to settle some scores. Robin Hughes' remarks from across the week are discussed, before the winner of the competition is revealed

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Elderly cheetah Matrah is ostracised by his brothers Burba and Singha when he starts to show signs of a leg injury. Cameras also follow Chester Zoo's Sumatran orangutans, including adolescent female Tuti and four-month-old Kesuma. And there's also a look at Asian pond turtle Shrek, whose love life proved fatal for a previous mate

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer visit the Alcorn family in Peebles in the Scottish Borders. The family of four lives in a conservation area and while dad Dave loves their grey-stone Victorian villa for its picture-book charm, mum Amy despises its lack of practicality. With no off-road parking, no third bedroom and no downstairs bathroom - not to mention the small, draughty kitchen - she has quite a list. Would it be best to simply move on to one of the new properties picked out by Phil, or can Kirstie come up with a way of making their home work?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: A recent storm leaves Lee and Belinda's castle keep in need of urgent repairs - and Lee must face his fear of heights if he is to repair the hole in the roof

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Architect Henning Stummel and his partner Alice Dawson plan to convert a dilapidated joinery workshop in north London into a contemporary family home and office. They want to preserve the building's original steel frame, but to restore it they will have to take it down, which means carefully extracting it from the neighbours' walls without causing damage to their property - and that's just the start of the couple's problems. Kevin McCloud follows their progress

programme.title: Lost Pyramids of the Aztecs
programme.desc: Part one of two. Archaeologists unearth the remains of the ancient civilisation from beneath Mexico City, digging deeper than ever before to find out who the Aztecs were and how they built their remarkable empire. The first episode features an attempt to build a replica of a pyramid, as well as investigating the mysterious pyramid ruins being unearthed in the heart of Mexico City and a journey into a set of underground tunnels that have lain undisturbed for centuries

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Former boxer Paul, 22, has been stabbed in the face and arm with a broken bottle outside a nightclub in Kingston. As the team works to stop the bleeding and treat the patient's wounds, his girlfriend Naveena reflects on his sensitive side. Angela, 93, is brought to A&E by her daughter Elizabeth after suffering from blood loss and disorientation at her care home, while 70-year-old John arrives with his wife Francesca after falling while out running and dislocating his shoulder. To get John's pain under control quickly the medics give him ketamine, and the hallucinogenic side effects keep everyone entertained

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and numbers-quiz, inviting Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan to take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. Dr John Cooper Clarke joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Cameras capture the work of some of Britain's busiest air ambulance teams as they race to save lives. The helicopter medics cut short an appearance at a charity event in Suffolk to treat a dirtbiker who's crashed at speed. Sammy has a cardiac arrest while working at a windfarm, and Stephen breaks his thigh while off-road motorbiking in the Pennines

programme.title: Lost Pyramids of the Aztecs
programme.desc: Part one of two. Archaeologists unearth the remains of the ancient civilisation from beneath Mexico City, digging deeper than ever before to find out who the Aztecs were and how they built their remarkable empire. The first episode features an attempt to build a replica of a pyramid, as well as investigating the mysterious pyramid ruins being unearthed in the heart of Mexico City and a journey into a set of underground tunnels that have lain undisturbed for centuries

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the most difficult cases he has taken on since the series began. First, he returns to Cafe Hon in Baltimore, Maryland, where owner Denise Whiting was trapped in a nasty battle with the community and had threatened the locals with lawsuits. Next, the chef goes back to Chiarella's in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. When he first visited owners Tommy and Dina DeFino, the business was struggling so badly that Tommy had completely lost his confidence and wife Dina had lost her patience. On his final stop, Gordon travels to Leone's in Montclair, New Jersey, where Rose Leone was depending on her lazy son Michael to run the restaurant

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads to Sicily, where a vast network of tunnels leads to a subterranean salt-processing plant. Kate Quilton looks at free range hens, and is surprised to learn that most chicks on British farms have their beaks trimmed at birth. In the Netherlands, she visits a new cutting-edge farm designed using the latest scientific advances in hen welfare. Plus, in Scotland, Matt Tebbutt makes a surprising discovery about the origins of haggis, and then faces the ordeal of delivering some unwelcome news to an unsuspecting audience

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Cadiz in Spain, where he samples two of the city's most revered culinary stars - fresh tuna and Manzanilla sherry. Rick is captivated by the narrow winding streets as he walks in the footsteps of the ancient Phoenician and Arab traders, whose influence on the city can still be seen to this day. Back home, he prepares flamenco eggs and arroz verde, or green rice, made with garlic, parsley, clams and prawns, producing the vibrant colours of the Spanish flag

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Coverage of the offbeat event which comes from the Cauberg, a hill in the southern town of Valkenburg, Holland

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May each buy a rear-wheel-drive coupe for £1,500 and find themselves entered into a French ice race. Richard discovers a way to fill the waiting time for flights by inventing the sport of airport-vehicle racing, Jeremy gets behind the wheel of the BMW Z4, and actress Sienna Miller is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: The Next Karate Kid (1994)
programme.desc: Wise karate mentor Mr Miyagi meets the orphaned teenage grand-daughter of a soldier who once saved his life. As well as guiding her through the trials of high school and her first romance, he takes her to a Zen monastery to hone her martial arts skills. Adventure sequel, starring Pat Morita, Hilary Swank, Michael Ironside and Constance Towers

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: A group of comedians completes a series of tasks. This time, Sally Phillips faces an amorous water cooler, and Aisling Bea crams a dish into a balloon

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Aisling Bea leaps cushions, while Bob Mortimer educates us on Dumfries and Galloway

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: In the final episode, Paul and Bob fish for Pike on the River Test in Hampshire, which may not be the best idea for two men of a certain age with heart problems. In the cottage where the pair are staying, they decide to invite a consultant cardiologist for dinner to talk about their medical past and their future, gaining tips on how to live the most healthy life possible. As the sun sets on the final night of their fishing expedition, Paul and Bob decide to write a eulogy for each on the riverbank

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by space scientist Maggie Aderin-Pocock, comedian Phil Wang, actor Jason Watkins and EastEnders star Adam Woodyatt, aiming to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: A compilation of extra material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests including Claudia Winkleman, Miles Jupp, Bob Mortimer, Jo Brand, Adil Ray, Kirsty Wark, Amanda Abbington and Carol Vorderman to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You

programme.desc: Cheeky Londoner Lee Nelson plays host for a 'well good' session of stand-up from the Hammersmith Apollo in London, warming up the audience before introducing routines by Canadian funnyman Stewart Francis and observational comic Paul Chowdhry

programme.desc: The guys pick the collection of a reclusive and mysterious businessman named 'the Colonel', who has gone to great lengths to keep his identity secret

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon and Lori are confused by a 3D printer, but are delighted with the valuation, while Papa Bear bids on two mysterious lots from Brazil

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys go hog wild at a sprawling ranch in Texas, where Mike flips for a VW bus that has been transformed into a hunting lodge on wheels

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys uncover a former firefighter's stockpile, which includes an ultra-rare piece of Space Age history. Plus, Frank's plan goes up in smoke

programme.title: Scrapyard Supercar
programme.desc: Engineer Ralph Hosier leads two teams of car buffs in their bid to build scrap cars. This time, they're up against pro driver Shane Lynch's Chevrolet Camaro in a plane-pulling contest. Building scrap able to get the 40-ton monster rolling will require innovation and a super-sized imagination.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets to grips with a Peel P50, the smallest car to go into production, and finds a surprising number of uses for it. James May experiences the luxury of the Rolls-Royce Phantom Drophead Coupe convertible, and the Stig puts the Ferrari 599 GTB Fiorano through its paces on the test track. The Rolling Stones' Ronnie Wood is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May find the fastest way to get around London - bike, boat or car - in a race through the capital during rush hour. The Stig takes to the test track in the Caparo T1, the Aston Martin V8 Vantage Roadster is pitted against a man with three rockets strapped to his back, and Simon Cowell is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The connoisseur of local food takes a gastronomic tour along the canal systems of southern France, from Bordeaux to Marseille. He samples seafood and regional cuisine, as well as exploring a variety of traditional recipes, including artichoke salad with poached egg, eels in cider, and bass with beurre blanc

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The connoisseur of local food explores the banks of the River Garonne on board a 100-year-old converted barge, and savours the delights of French provincial cooking including magret de canard and petit sale. Other dishes featured are clams in white garlic sauce, eels in parsley and garlic butter, and steak tartare

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: James May straps into a highly tuned hatchback as he competes in a world rallycross race, with Top Gear USA host and professional driver Tanner Foust teaming up with him. Jeremy Clarkson puts the latest Jaguar F-type R and Eagle's modernised E-type the Low Drag GT though their paces, while Richard Hammond is in Spain to test the new version of the Mazda MX-5. Plus, X-Men: First Class star Nicholas Hoult takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.desc: Documentary featuring dramatic reconstructions and interviews with historians to examine the network of spymasters and secret agents that helped protect Elizabeth I from assassination, terror and treason for more than 40 years. The first edition reveals how the pope's declaration of Elizabeth as a heretic 12 years into her reign set England's Catholics against her, leading the queen to charge William Cecil with the task of stopping assassins getting through, and the huge espionage network he established saw his spies break Catholic conspiracies at home and abroad

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Drama-documentary revealing the most shocking and surprising murder cases in history. At the time of Jack the Ripper, London was home to some of the most terrible individuals the city has ever seen. One of the very worst was the elusive Borough Poisoner, George Chapman. Presenter Nicholas Day reveals how he was finally brought to justice

programme.title: War Factories

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through the Lake District, where he discovers a magical world of talking rabbits, ducks, hedgehogs and mice, who have entertained children for more than 100 years. At the village home of author and illustrator Beatrix Potter, he learns about her legacy and her fears about the railways. Michael presses on to Brantwood, home of the Victorian art critic, John Ruskin, and finishes with a brief encounter at Carnforth

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reads the riot act in Preston, and discovers four mill workers were shot dead at a protest in 1842. In Darwen, he traces developments in interior design from wallpaper to paint, before exploring the Victorian industrial landscape of Salford in the paintings on LS Lowry. He finishes his leg of the journey on Kersal Moor, where he discovers the poetry of Edwin Waugh and twists his tongue around the Lancashire dialect

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Arley Hall and Gardens in Cheshire, where a time capsule that has been buried for 130 years is opened for the first time on camera. programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built

programme.title: Secrets of Britain
programme.desc: Exploring the history of Westminster Abbey, which dates back to the 11th century and houses more than 500 corpses, including those of Edward I, Elizabeth I, Charles Darwin and Isaac Newton, as well as staging 16 royal weddings. The episode also explores the Houses of Parliament and Big Ben

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A vast project in the icy wilderness of southern Alaska, and a landscape of confusing and surprising structures that represented a bold statement of Spain's plans for the future

programme.title: Nazi Victory: The Post-War Plan
programme.desc: How Hitler used recreation to promote his ideology, setting up the world's largest beach resort and travel agency and attempting to turn the Olympics into a propaganda tool

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: How the Americans, once they were provoked into war, out-produced the rest of the world so quickly and by such a huge margin, including the likes of General Motors

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from St Helens to Knutsford, finding out about modern glass-making and how techniques invented in the Victorian era to construct buildings such as the Crystal Palace have evolved and are powering a new architectural revolution

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey through north-west England, Michael Portillo makes a clean sweep in Ashley, Cheshire, where in Victorian times the new middle classes set up home in suburban villas with multiple chimneys swept by children. In Macclesfield, he finds the end of the Silk Route and tries his hand at screen printing. Then after stoking the fire on the steam-powered Churnet Valley Railway, the former politician alights at Froghall, Staffordshire, heading for Alton Towers to trace the 19th-century origins of the theme park

programme.title: Trains That Changed the World

Beginning in the port of Dover, he takes a plunge into the English Channel, inspired by the example set by a brave 19th-century sea captain. A miniature steam train one third the size of a conventional locomotive conveys him from Romney Marsh to Dungeness, and in Eastbourne, he learns how the 7th Duke of Devonshire managed to market the town's attractions to the refined upper-crust of Victorian London. The first leg of his trip concludes in eccentric style at the Glyndebourne opera festival on the South Downs

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Newhaven to Worthing, examining the nation's hidden defences, and taking in two magnificent engineering achievements - the Ouse Valley Viaduct and the Clayton Tunnel. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Arvada, Colorado, where a husband and wife have allowed their restaurant to fall into disrepair. Randy's hoarding is out of control, while Alexa, who runs the kitchen, has a lot to learn. To make matters worse, the Old Neighborhood is one of the filthiest eateries the fiery chef has ever seen, and only one of the biggest transformations of the series can save the business

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Part one of two. Hard-nosed owner Julie finds herself completely at odds with the head chef, forcing her to breaking point

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay concludes his two-part challenge at Mangia Mangia in Woodland Park, Colorado. In order to save the struggling Italian restaurant from disaster, he must persuade harassed owner Julie to co-operate with the head chef, and work his magic on a menu that relies heavily on a microwave

programme.title: Sarah Millican: Control Enthusiast
programme.desc: A stand-up performance by the comedian, including topics such as road rage, IBS, her favourite word and how she lets her husband know 'tonight's the night'. There are also stories about 'Rescue Men', passing wind in hospital pants, what can happen at a bra fitting and how she feels about her belly

programme.title: Lodgers for Codgers
programme.desc: The chances of a 25 to 35-year-old person owning a home have halved in the last 20 years, while millions of over-sixties are sitting on empty rooms, so here young and old tackle Britain's housing crisis by trialling a unique solution - moving in together. In this third instalment, dog groomer Liah-Rose moves home and business to Reading and pensioner landlords Carolynne and David's high-octane lifestyle. Meanwhile, Jake from Middlesbrough heads for 61-year-old Christene's place in Pontefract, West Yorkshire

programme.title: Council House Britain
programme.desc: The work of frontline council workers in Southwark, south London, one of the biggest housing associations in the country. Housing officer Cheryl is chasing down a tenant who owes more than £10,000 in rent. She suspects he could be subletting but needs to be sure before she begins the eviction process. Pest controller Andy climbs into a loft space that has been invaded by pigeons, which can cause enormous damage, and an elderly woman with cancer is desperate for her and her husband to find somewhere better than the unsuitable bedsit they currently call home

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Documentary following life inside an A&E department, focusing on patients and their families, as well as the work of the staff dealing with the emergencies. This episode follows the stories of three patients and how they cope when life takes an unexpected turn, including a patient who severed three of his fingers with an electric saw. Elsewhere, a man with motor neurone disease is rushed to St George's with severe sepsis, and a teenager struggles to come to terms with the impact of serious injuries he sustained in a car crash six weeks ago

programme.title: Bring Back the Bush: Where Did Our Pubic Hair Go?
programme.desc: Author Chidera Eggerue explores people's relationship with pubic hair, investigating why so many women remove their pubic hair when even a few decades ago it wasn't seen as necessary. Along the way, she challenges herself and her peers to grow out their pubic hair and put on an exhibition in which they reveal their naked, natural bodies to the world

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Veterinary surgeon James Emson hosts the second party in Darlington, Co Durham, and decides to accompany his curry main course with a gruesome contest in which he serves up a selection of eyeballs, tongues and other bits of offal, then challenges his guests to identify which animal they came from. Diner Barry l'Anson also manages to leave the party having achieved a lifetime ambition

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Barry I'Anson hosts the third dinner party in Darlington, Co Durham, and hopes his love-themed evening will endear him to his guests. Unfortunately for him Clair Sherwood-Parkin is not impressed and begins doubting the host's masculinity, and vet James Emson is concerned what the dessert will be like. Charlotte Langley finds herself wanting a sick bag before the evening is over, and Eseyoma Sodje steals the show during some cringe-worthy entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas shows five properties to Greater Manchester couple Sandra and Robert, who want a holiday home on Spain's Costa Blanca with plenty of storage space for their bikes and located within a short walk to the beach

programme.title: George Clarke's National Trust Unlocked
programme.desc: George visits the magnificent and meticulously crafted country retreat of Standen in West Sussex - one of the best examples of the Arts and Crafts design movement to be found anywhere in the world. He discovers a masterpiece of Gothic Revival architecture at Tyntesfield in Somerset, with its acres of flower-filled terraces, and unleashes a medieval catapult at Corfe Castle in Dorset, an iconic ruin who's first stone was laid almost a millennium ago, before risking vertigo with his trusty companion Loki at the highest point on England's south coast - the famous Golden Cap

programme.title: Call the Cops
programme.desc: A look at two very different manhunts involving the Devon and Cornwall police. The first case involves a car which flipped off the road while travelling at over 100mph and hit a tree. When officers arrive at the scene, they discover that the driver and passengers have fled the scene. A 17-strong team, with armed support, a dog unit and aerial assets, ventures into the surrounding woodland to discover where they have gone. Control also receives a call to be on the lookout for a missing man who is reported to be suicidal and whose whereabouts are uncertain

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics return to share their opinions on shows including (Un)well, When Harry Met Sally, Countdown, Britain's Got Talent, Mary Berry's Simple Comforts, Elephant Hospital, BBC news reports on quarantine, and young people and Covid, and ITV news coverage of Brexit

programme.title: The Yorkshire Jobcentre
programme.desc: This episode of the documentary follows Ray, a 63-year-old piano tuner who has been coming to the Jobcentre for two years, but his struggles with technology are making his job search difficult to keep up with. Suraya used to work in beauty, before leaving to be a full-time mum for 16 years. Now that her children are grown up, the 48-year-old wants to get back into the industry, but it has evolved more than she'd imagined. Elsewhere, 50-year-old Neil was made redundant eight months ago and is using his savings to get by. He does voluntary work in the community, but needs a paid job to cover his mortgage

programme.title: Superhuman Summer: The Paralympic Rewind
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas and Jonnie Peacock present a celebration of some of the most memorable moments from the 2012 and 2016 Paralympic Games in London and Rio. With guests including Clare Balding, Ade Adepitan, Alex Brooker, Adam Hills and Josh Widdicombe, as well as members of Britain's Paralympics team

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: With passenger numbers sky rocketing, airports and planes are increasingly becoming pressurised environments. Footage captured by people who were there highlights some of the most dramatic, extraordinary and unusual moments that UK passengers have experienced. From terrible behaviour in the terminal and tantrums on the tarmac to full-scale brawls on board, the programme hears from the people who were involved. Expert analysis from industry insiders and psychologists explains why people behave how they do on flights from hell

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer continue to celebrate two decades of the property programme by looking back at the times they have been asked to search for million-pound houses. But with a big budget comes big expectations, as proved by Steve and Dee, who had a list of 34 requirements and a tiny search area. Claire was the programme's youngest ever house-hunter. Needing full-time help due to having cerebral palsy, she wanted an accessible but glamorous central London pad. Meanwhile, Bermondsey boy Matt and fiancee Tricia hoped to start a family in their dream rural Kentish home with scenic views

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. Meanwhile, a rescue dog has a cancerous tumour in his jaw removed, and a young staffie-cross needs treatment for a developmental elbow disease

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Taxi driver Nik Christides hosts the first party in east Hampshire, where one guest causes a stir by revealing details of a romantic encounter with Boyzone singer Ronan Keating. Later, Nik introduces his diners to the sport of Cheesy Darts, which requires one guest to smear their face with cream, before having cheese-flavoured crisps thrown at them

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-four Jax Naudi hosts the second dinner party in east Hampshire, but her theme for the evening causes confusion and leads to one guest wearing an inappropriate outfit. Although Sue Hood expresses her distaste for the host's menu by remaining silent throughout the meal, the mood improves when Jax reveals the evening's entertainment - life drawing with a nude model

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Natalie Edemska-Plant hosts the third dinner party in east Hampshire, and plans to serve dishes from her childhood to give her guests an authentic Russian dining experience. She begins the evening with traditional customs such as banning shoes inside the house, and although her food is not a complete success, the night ends on a high note with some energetic dancing

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: History fanatic Kyle Curtis hosts the fourth dinner party from east Hampshire. A self-confessed novice in the kitchen, he decides to get his guests to help him with his main course - a fish fondue - and his choice of dress code for the evening proves a hit

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sue Hood hosts the final night of the culinary competition in east Hampshire, hoping to impress her guests with a Caribbean-themed dinner party. The evening kicks off with the diners taking turns hitting a pinata, followed by entertainment in the form of dancers before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Rita and Stephen from Watford find a holiday home in the Portuguese town of Albufeira, showing them five options within their budget of £150,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: With retirement rapidly approaching, Jan and Phil Jackson wish to relocate to the Canary Island of Fuerteventura, having been inspired by Jan's sister who has already made the move there. Property expert Scarlette Douglas shows them five detached villas within their £215,000 budget

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the pet-friendly Dog in a Doublet in Peterborough, where Della McGinn's passion for animals comes a close second to husband John's passion for food

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien turns car seats into a three-piece suite during a trip to Leyland, while Henry Cole repurposes an overhead light into a fashionable light box

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert contemplates leaving the police force to take up a job selling security systems, but the proposal receives a mixed reaction, leading to tense celebrations at his birthday party. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Brad Garrett and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is delighted to be awarded an honorary doctorate, but hurts Debra's feelings by thanking everyone except her in his acceptance speech. He claims it was an accident, though she takes the opportunity to remind him of past snubs that were obviously deliberate

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to New Orleans to visit Zeke's, a much-loved restaurant that has seen standards slip since the original owner died. The new proprietors, husband and wife duo Darryl and Ellen Cortello, focused all the efforts on the financial side of things, so it is up to Gordon to show them that they first need to love food to make any profit

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay concludes his two-part challenge at Mangia Mangia in Woodland Park, Colorado. In order to save the struggling Italian restaurant from disaster, he must persuade harassed owner Julie to co-operate with the head chef, and work his magic on a menu that relies heavily on a microwave

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin assists Joanna and Scott, who met 18 years ago in southern Spain while they were on a golfing trip, and are now searching for a home on the Costa Blanca

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps a couple from the West Midlands with three children to find a holiday home in Tenerife, tracking down five properties within their budget of £185,000

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer continue to celebrate two decades of the property programme by looking back at the times they have been asked to search for million-pound houses. But with a big budget comes big expectations, as proved by Steve and Dee, who had a list of 34 requirements and a tiny search area. Claire was the programme's youngest ever house-hunter. Needing full-time help due to having cerebral palsy, she wanted an accessible but glamorous central London pad. Meanwhile, Bermondsey boy Matt and fiancee Tricia hoped to start a family in their dream rural Kentish home with scenic views

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics return to share their opinions on shows including (Un)well, When Harry Met Sally, Countdown, Britain's Got Talent, Mary Berry's Simple Comforts, Elephant Hospital, BBC news reports on quarantine, and young people and Covid, and ITV news coverage of Brexit

programme.title: Council House Britain
programme.desc: The work of frontline council workers in Southwark, south London, one of the biggest housing associations in the country. Housing officer Cheryl is chasing down a tenant who owes more than £10,000 in rent. She suspects he could be subletting but needs to be sure before she begins the eviction process. Pest controller Andy climbs into a loft space that has been invaded by pigeons, which can cause enormous damage, and an elderly woman with cancer is desperate for her and her husband to find somewhere better than the unsuitable bedsit they currently call home

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: James Blunt, Alison Hammond, Alex Jones and Joe Sugg take part in the charity culinary challenge, under the watchful eye of presenters Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding, and judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith. For the signature round, they must prepare a batch of decorated traybakes, while the technical challenge involves a classic British staple. Finally, for the Showstopper, they must create a scene out of biscuits depicting what they wanted to be when they grew up

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Compilation of reports on store cupboard staples, including Kate Quilton taking a look at the secrets behind peanut butter's long shelf life, solving the mysteries of Marmite's famously divisive flavour, and discovering if it is safe to feed cats dog food and vice versa. Jimmy Doherty travels to Sri Lanka to discover why lentils are sold split rather than whole, and Matt Tebbutt witnesses anchovy fishing off the coast of Italy

programme.title: Lodgers for Codgers
programme.desc: The chances of a 25 to 35-year-old person owning a home have halved in the last 20 years, while millions of over-sixties are sitting on empty rooms, so here young and old tackle Britain's housing crisis by trialling a unique solution - moving in together. In this third instalment, dog groomer Liah-Rose moves home and business to Reading and pensioner landlords Carolynne and David's high-octane lifestyle. Meanwhile, Jake from Middlesbrough heads for 61-year-old Christene's place in Pontefract, West Yorkshire

programme.title: The Black Full Monty
programme.desc: Documentary going on the road with The Chocolate Men, Britain's first all-black touring strip dancing group. Filmed during their 2018 nationwide tour, the film meets the managers Louis and Dante - who thought up the idea after hosting a Magic Mike-themed show - and explores what motivates each of the dancers to bare all night after night

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: With passenger numbers sky rocketing, airports and planes are increasingly becoming pressurised environments. Footage captured by people who were there highlights some of the most dramatic, extraordinary and unusual moments that UK passengers have experienced. From terrible behaviour in the terminal and tantrums on the tarmac to full-scale brawls on board, the programme hears from the people who were involved. Expert analysis from industry insiders and psychologists explains why people behave how they do on flights from hell

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Steve have an emotional confrontation in Berkshire when a tearful woman accuses her landlord of fleecing her for extra rent, while Brian and Del travel to Luton to recover £4,500 owed by an elusive debtor who has been giving them the runaround

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, father-and-son team Del and Dael Anglin face death threats from a debtor's father, after arriving at a property to collect a debt of over £3,500. Meanwhile, in east London, two agents are confronted with a tenant who claims to have had no prior warning that he is being evicted from his tiny bedsit. Del and Brian try to recover £6,000 owed by a London hotel to a catering supply company, and Paul and Steve must tackle a difficult eviction

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In north London, agents Del and Dael chase nearly £2,000 owed by a printing company, but the owner denies the debt and starts getting physical. Meanwhile, Stewart and Elmor find themselves caught up in a personal dispute when they try to collect rent arrears from a barber's shop, and in Bedfordshire, Del and Brian try to recover almost £2,000 owed to a funeral director. Stewart and Vic track down a debt of £5,000 from the owner of a computer repair shop in Lincolnshire

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor visit Nottingham to chase almost £8,000 owed by a car dealership to a dissatisfied customer, while on the Isle of Wight, Brian and Del try to collect £3,700 of unpaid estate agency fees. The job begins peacefully, until one of the agents is physically assaulted by the debtor's wife. In Lancashire, Stewart and Iain struggle to identify the person who owes almost £2,000 after a failed compensation claim, while Del and Dael try to recover nearly £3,500 from a businessman in west London

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, Del and Dael Anglin receive a hostile reception from the brother of a debtor who has alreasy defaulted on a promise to pay £2,000 she owes to a storage company. In Worcestershire, Gareth and Craig try to recover £45,000 from a young online entrepreneur, who claims no responsibility for the debt he owes to his courier. Their target cooks up a flimsy lie in an effort to absolve himself, but dishonesty proves to be the least of the agents' worries when they discover a stash of guns at the back of the garage. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a £4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The buy-to-let documentary series looks back at cases from earlier episodes to find out if matters were resolved or have only got worse. They include a Yorkshire landlord who had to call in eviction specialists to remove tenants from the house he inherited from his grandparents, and a bus driver who managed to win back possession of his home from a nightmare tenant. But just as the eviction was going through, she threatened to call the police

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Updates on the story of property developer Jon Morgan, who called in an eviction specialist when the disused tram station he bought as an investment was taken over by squatters. Plus, a couple who rented out their home while they moved to America - only for their tenant to end up owing them £10,000 in back payments - and how a team from Peterborough Council came across a house with life-threatening electrics and some of the worst damp and mould they had ever seen

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: People featured in previous episodes, including an Essex landlady who wondered why her tenant had stopped paying rent - only to discover that the woman was in prison for GBH and that her ex-boyfriend had moved into the flat. programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: On Benefits
programme.desc: The documentary about welfare recipients follows people with big dreams of getting back into work, including an ex-soldier whose life was turned upside down after a false accusation of theft. Now he is rebuilding his life and saving as much money as he can as he tries to turn his interest in photography into a career as a photojournalist. The film also features a 48-year-old grandmother who dreams of getting back into security work, and a former salesman with a dream of making it in the music business

programme.title: On Benefits
programme.desc: The documentary follows 34-year-old Shaun from Stockton-on-Tees who has been on and off benefits - and in and out of trouble with the police - since leaving school, and is finally trying to turn his life around. Leon from Ashton-under-Lyme is fed up of only finding jobs with zero-hours contracts but cannot afford the transport to look further afield, while Knowsley-based cab driver Mark has not worked since having his licence revoked following an angina attack two years ago. Now he struggles to manage the stairs to his flat and needs moving to a new home

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

As tempers rise, the pair must find a way to handle a situation that is spiralling dangerously out of control. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase £3,000 owed to a firm of letting agents, before tracking a debtor who was accidentally over-paid for a job. Plus, Stewart and Iain face an eviction that is set to test their patience

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Steve are sent to north London to evict a young Romanian mother who owes £6,000 in unpaid rent, but when they arrive, they discover a family home that is not quite what it appears to be. Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than £7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a £400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: In Huddersfield, officers are in pursuit of a van driver who smashed his van into a parked car before fleeing, while Bob and Dan pepper spray a suspect who is not keen on being handcuffed. programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Columbo: A Trace of Murder (1997)
programme.desc: A conniving woman yearns to divorce her husband and set up home with her lover - but a pre-nuptial agreement means she stands to lose out. Instead, she devises a cunning plan to nullify the contract, framing her unsuspecting spouse for the murder of a business rival. However, she reckons without the shabby sleuth's unfailing ability to uncover the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Shera Danese, Barry Corbin and David Rasche

programme.title: Columbo Likes the Nightlife (2003)
programme.desc: The disheveled cop is drawn into the world of the Los Angeles club scene when he investigates the apparent suicide of a tabloid reporter, and uncovers evidence of a blackmail plot. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Matthew Rhys and Jennifer Sky

programme.title: Columbo: An Exercise in Fatality (1974)
programme.desc: The owner of a chain of health clubs kills a business partner who was about to expose his crooked dealings. Making the death look like an unfortunate weightlifting accident, he cunningly creates the perfect alibi using a tape recording of his victim's voice - but he reckons without the intervention of the shabby sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Conrad and Philip Bruns

programme.title: Columbo: Agenda for Murder (1990)
programme.desc: A crooked lawyer hopes to start a promising political career, but a former client he saved from prison threatens to blow the whistle on him. The attorney murders the blackmailer and makes it look like suicide - but the shabby sleuth sees through his ruse and sets out to expose the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Louis Zorich and Patrick McGoohan, who also directed

programme.title: Columbo: Butterfly in Shades of Grey (1993)
programme.desc: A radio presenter is plotting to sabotage his adopted daughter's plans to publish her first novel and kills one of his employees for trying to help her. He thinks he has the perfect alibi, claiming that the victim was murdered while they were talking to each other on the phone, and there is a tape recording to prove it - but his ruse fails to fool the dishevelled detective. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, William Shatner and Molly Hagan

programme.title: Columbo: Rest in Peace, Mrs Columbo (1990)
programme.desc: The wife of a criminal who died in prison swears revenge on the men she holds responsible for his incarceration - including Columbo. She kills one of her targets, and the shabby sleuth is assigned to the case. As she faces his well-honed approach to sniffing out a suspect, she plots to murder his wife. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Helen Shaver and Ian McShane

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team searches for a petty officer suspected of assault, who escapes when the van transporting him crashes into a lake. Torres works with a witness who heard vital evidence

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The discovery of an African boy's dismembered body leads the detectives to suspect he was killed by cultists as a ritual sacrifice - but subsequent investigations implicate a slave-trading ring operating from Nigeria. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Drama, guest starring Jane Seymour, with Mariska Hargitay and Christopher Meloni

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Benson enlist the help of Dr Huang as they struggle to find a connection between a major pharmaceutical firm's illegal marketing campaign and the fatal shooting of two high-school students. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and BD Wong

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A famous rock star is acquitted of raping a fan after the victim's claims are rejected. Logan and Briscoe decide to investigate further, and discover the musician's pleasant reputation does not quite match his personality. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A baby is found dead in his cot, and reports indicate he was poisoned. All the evidence pins the blame on a British au pair employed by the parents, but she claims the infant's death was a result of the mother's negligence. Sam Waterston stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe, Curtis, McCoy and Kincaid witness the execution of a criminal they helped bring to justice, but the event culminates in personal tragedies for each of them

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A decomposed skeleton is unearthed on a construction site and identification proves troublesome for Stone. The deceased is eventually revealed to be a man who disappeared years earlier and was presumed murdered. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Fontana and Green track down a disturbed woman who has not only murdered a young mother and stolen her five-day-old son, but also managed to convince her own husband that the baby is really theirs. As the case goes to trial, another man comes forward claiming to be the child's real father, leaving McCoy and Borgia facing a dilemma

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Green and Cassady look into the murder of Carl Mullaly, who had recently been profiled on HardFocus, a magazine programme that identifies sex offenders. The only witness is an eight-year-old girl and detectives soon make an arrest, but learn the tabloid talk show may have been more involved than it initially appeared

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine suspected of murdering a girl is killed in Iraq, and a search of his personal belongings reveals her body. Most members of the team believe they have found conclusive evidence of his guilt - but Gibbs suspects there is more to the situation than meets the eye

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is infected with a deadly virus sent through the post by shadowy figures who demand the details of a long-closed case be made public in exchange for the antidote. McGee and Gibbs set out to identify the culprits and find a cure for the disease. Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. The team reels in the wake of the terrorist attack, but tries to avenge Kate's death by collecting as much information as possible on Ari before he does any more damage. Meanwhile, Gibbs learns one of his old flames has been named as the new NCIS director

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gibbs continues to blame himself for Kate's death, and enlists Jenny's help to track down Ari - but they are nearly killed in a shoot-out. Just when all seems lost, however, Ziva is able to point the investigation in the right direction. Starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sara Carter begs Gibbs to investigate her mother's murder, as she believes the local authorities have convicted the wrong man. Mark Harmon, Pauley Perrette and Skye P Marshall star

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

The police take the youngster into protective custody, but a lack of evidence forces them to return him home - with tragic consequences. Crime drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An actress is found hanged in her New York apartment, but with her career on the rise and a loving husband supporting her, Green and Cassady are determined to prove the death was not the suicide it appears to be. Crime drama, starring Jesse L Martin and Milena Govich

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The ex-wife of a respected former senator is found murdered in her home, and Green and Cassady's inquiries lead them to a dysfunctional family with a number of strange secrets. programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Basildon, Essex, to meet nine-year-old Brandon, who suffers a severe form of epilepsy and can have up to 30 seizures a day. He requires 24-hour care and mum Jenny can't let him out of her sight for a moment - and to make matters worse, the family home simply isn't safe enough for the youngster. Luckily, the local tradesmen have heard of the family's plight and arrive in force to help Nick and the team transform the home, with designer Naomi Cleaver on hand to ensure they don't stray too far from her vision

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Swansea to meet 15-year-old Jack, who suffers from a rare form of muscular dystrophy that has led to a lifetime of painful operations. His mother Helen and older brother Daniel do all they can to help but as he grows it has become impossible to get him upstairs and now he has to sleep in the living room, leaving the rest of the family with nowhere to socialise. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their three-bedroom semi, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to ensure they don't stray too far from her vision

programme.title: Walks Around Britain

programme.title: Daddy Daycare
programme.desc: Three men are sent to work at a north-west London nursery, where father-of-four Grant receives a dressing down from manager Marion after losing his temper with the children. Paul is convinced childcare is easy and his partner Sally is eager for him to understand just how difficult it is, while recruitment consultant Tony's wife Marilyn hopes the experience will prompt him to spend less time working and watching sport and more time with his baby son

programme.title: The Cat Gang (1959)
programme.desc: A gang of children is always hanging around a harbour - although the customs officers are always trying to get rid of them. When the youngsters stumble on some shady characters behaving suspiciously, they end up uncovering a smuggling plot. Children's crime drama, starring Francesca Annis and John Pike

programme.title: Skid Kids (1953)
programme.desc: A group of kids takes on a gang of bike thieves, with a little help from the police. Children's drama, starring Barry MacGregor and Anthony Lang

programme.title: Three Steps in the Dark (1953)
programme.desc: A wealthy eccentric invites all the members of his family to his country house, so he can discuss the contents of his Will. However, when he is suddenly killed, everyone present is a suspect. Murder mystery, starring Greta Gynt and Hugh Sinclair

programme.title: Dear Murderer (1947)
programme.desc: A man believes he has committed the perfect crime when he kills his wife's two lovers in a jealous rage. Melodrama, starring Eric Portman, Dennis Price, Jack Warner, Greta Gynt and Hazel Court

programme.title: Far from the Madding Crowd (1967)
programme.desc: A beautiful woman arouses the ardour of three rival suitors in 19th-century rural Dorset and, having flirted and toyed with their affections, she chooses to marry a caddish, gambling soldier - a decision that leads to a sequence of harrowing events. John Schlesinger's adaptation of Thomas Hardy's classic novel, with Julie Christie, Alan Bates, Terence Stamp and Peter Finch

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Featuring inspirational walks from around Britain

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps Wiltshire couple and pop opera fanatics Nikki Kedge and Angela Rawson find a bigger home in their dream location of Scotland's west coast. Desperate for more room to put on plays for local children, they view six houses in the county of Dumfries and Galloway - but will there be anything that tickles their fancy?

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Members of the British Resistance Movement recall 'the most vivid days' of their lives and Peter Williams tells the story of the Werewolves, the Nazi fanatics who tried to form a German underground resistance

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement men who parachuted with the SAS into France on D-Day and the massacre of 35 men in a French woodland

programme.title: What Music Means

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath

programme.title: Hardware
programme.desc: Mike steadfastly resists a management attempt to make the staff wear identical uniforms, convinced it's a move designed to rob them of their individuality _ but a visit from Anne's surprisingly attractive mother soon diverts his attention to more personal matters. Comedy, starring Martin Freeman, Susan Earl and Ken Morley

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.desc: A Polish Holocaust survivor tries to start afresh in 1940s New York, but is tormented by ghosts from her nightmarish past, leaving her desperately seeking solace in a stormy relationship with an unpredictable chemist. But all is not as it seems, as a new neighbour seems set to discover. Drama, starring an Oscar-winning Meryl Streep alongside Kevin Kline and Peter MacNicol

programme.title: Damage (1992)
programme.desc: An English politician at the height of his career risks everything he holds dear when he gives in to his impulses and recklessly embarks on a passionate relationship with his son's girlfriend. When news of the affair reaches Whitehall, the fallout looks set to end his political ambitions. Drama, starring Jeremy Irons, Juliette Binoche, Rupert Graves, and Miranda Richardson

The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Manchester City v Sunderland. Highlights of the showpiece occasion at Wembley Stadium, where the first major domestic silverware of the 2013/14 season was decided. City started as clear favourites as they continued their quest for trophies, but the Black Cats could take heart from their impressive recent record against their big-spending opponents, albeit in home matches, and the fact that they had already eliminated Chelsea and Manchester United from this season's competition

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Bradford City v Northampton Town. Action from the 2012/13 League Two play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where promotion to League One awaited the winners. The Bantams were making their second appearance of the season at Wembley having also reached the League Cup final following an outstanding giant-killing run, and they needed to come from behind to edge out Burton Albion to reach this stage. The Cobblers ended a run of recent poor form to win 1-0 in both legs of their semi-final against Cheltenham Town to move within one match of reaching the third tier of English football

programme.desc: A look back at the 2012 Major at the Olympic Club in San Francisco, California, where defending champion Rory McIlroy missed the cut, while the likes of Graeme McDowell, Webb Simpson, Jim Furyk and Padraig Harrington put themselves in contention going into the final round

programme.title: US Open Official Films
programme.desc: A look back at the 2013 Major at the Merion Golf Club in Ardmore, Pennsylvania, where Justin Rose went on to claim the title. That victory saw Rose become the first English player to win this tournament since Tony Jacklin in 1970, and the first to win any of the Majors since Nick Faldo triumphed at the Masters in 1996
